name: Abatwa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth)
environment: The Eternal Steppe (open grasslands, anthills, rocky uplands near Warg territory)
organization: Solitary, pair, hunting band (3–8), or tribe (20–60 with 1d4 ant mounts per 10 Abatwa)
description: Crouched in the shadow of a single blade of steppe-grass, nearly invisible against the red earth, a figure no taller than a man’s thumb draws a bow strung with spider-silk. Its arrow — a splinter of bone dipped in something that smells faintly of turned soil — is aimed with the steady confidence of a hunter who has never missed.

name: Aboleth
flags: elemental_affinity:None (Extraplanar — outside Oreka’s elemental system)
environment: Deep Apelian Sea (abyssal trenches, drowned enclaves below the Na’wasua shelf)
organization: Solitary, pair, or enclave (3–5 plus 2d10 enslaved thralls of various races)
description: The water darkens without cause. Far below, where the light of the surface is a rumour, something vast turns in the silt — a shape like a beached whale wrapped in mucus, its three vertical eyes glowing with a cold intelligence that did not originate in this world. Tentacles trail behind it like the trains of a drowned king’s robe. It came here with the Deceivers. It chose to stay. And it has been building ever since.

name: Adze
flags: elemental_affinity:None (Extraplanar — summoned from the Dómnathar’s home plane)
environment: Deepwater Marches (halfling settlements, marsh villages, river-trading posts)
description: The lamp at the window gutters. A firefly drifts in through the gap beneath the door — just one, glowing faintly amber in the dark. It lands on the sleeping child’s arm. By morning, the child is pale, feverish, and will not wake. The firefly is gone. There is no wound to find.

name: Air Elemental, Small
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Wind Lord (Air)
environment: Any windy locale; most common in the Eternal Steppe (storm-paths, open grasslands), Giant’s Teeth (high passes, summit winds), and Twin Rivers (Great Forest canopy during storms)
description: The wind gathers where no wind should be. Dust rises in a spiral, leaves whip into a column, and then it is there — a shape made of rushing air, its outline barely visible except where debris traces its form. It smells of high places: ozone, cold rain, the thin air above the mountaintops. It does not speak, but the sound it makes as it moves is like breathing drawn through a cathedral.

name: Air Elemental, Medium
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Wind Lord (Air)
environment: As Small Air Elemental
description: The wind gathers where no wind should be. Dust rises in a spiral, leaves whip into a column, and then it is there — a shape made of rushing air, its outline barely visible except where debris traces its form. It smells of high places: ozone, cold rain, the thin air above the mountaintops. It does not speak, but the sound it makes as it moves is like breathing drawn through a cathedral.

flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Wind Lord (Air)
environment: As Small Air Elemental
description: The wind gathers where no wind should be. Dust rises in a spiral, leaves whip into a column, and then it is there — a shape made of rushing air, its outline barely visible except where debris traces its form. It smells of high places: ozone, cold rain, the thin air above the mountaintops. It does not speak, but the sound it makes as it moves is like breathing drawn through a cathedral.

environment: As Small Air Elemental
description: The wind gathers where no wind should be. It smells of high places: ozone, cold rain, the thin air above the mountaintops. It does not speak, but the sound it makes as it moves is like breathing drawn through a cathedral.

environment: As Small Air Elemental
description: The wind gathers where no wind should be. name: Air Elemental, Huge
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Wind Lord (Air)
environment: As Small Air Elemental
description: The wind gathers where no wind should be. Dust rises in a spiral, leaves whip into a column, and then it is there — a shape made of rushing air, its outline barely visible except where debris traces its form. It smells of high places: ozone, cold rain, the thin air above the mountaintops. It does not speak, but the sound it makes as it moves is like breathing drawn through a cathedral.

name: Allip
flags: elemental_affinity:Fragmented — distorted remnant of original Kin’s elemental identity
environment: Kinsweave (border-kingdom ruins, abandoned guild halls, fallen court chambers); Tidebloom Reach (drowned villages near Hillmeet and Foxfen, waterlogged libraries). Rare in the Infinite Desert near collapsed Giant record-halls.
description: It was a scholar, once. You can tell by the way it moves its hands — still tracing letters on invisible pages, still turning invisible leaves. Its mouth opens and closes ceaselessly, pouring out a stream of whispered words that resolve into nothing, loop back on themselves, and begin again. The sound is not language. It is the sound of a mind that broke so completely that death could not stop it from trying to finish the thought.

name: Androsphinx
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) — dual affinity
environment: The Infinite Desert (Giant ruins, carved canyon approaches, exposed plateau-tops near the Sunken Thrones and Tharok’s Pillar); secondary presence in the Glass Wastes (ridgelines overlooking the sand-flats)
description: It sits on a slab of carved stone that was once a throne-room lintel, its lion’s body perfectly still, its human face turned toward the horizon as though watching for something that will never come. Sand drifts against its flanks. Its mane, thick as a horse’s tail, lifts in the desert wind. When it speaks — and it will speak, if it judges you worthy of words — its voice resonates in the chest like a struck bell, and the sand around it trembles.

name: Ankheg
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth)
environment: The Eternal Steppe (cultivated farmland and open grassland, especially along the Kinsweave border); secondary presence in Twin Rivers (tilled lowland fields near the Great River) and Tidebloom Reach (drained marshland converted to cropland near Hillmeet)
description: The wheat shivers and collapses in a line, stalks folding inward like a wound stitching itself shut. Then the earth cracks open and something huge and chitinous erupts — six legs driving it skyward, mandibles gaping, a hiss of acrid vapor rising from its mouth. It is the color of the soil it lives in, which means you never see it until the ground beneath you gives way.

name: Annis Hag
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — corrupted; see Society
environment: The Eternal Steppe (rocky foothills along the mountain borders, deep gullies, abandoned cave systems); secondary in Giant’s Teeth foothills (Vestri territory border) and the Kinsweave (forested highland valleys)
organization: Solitary or covey (3 hags of any type)
description: She is nine feet tall and could be mistaken for a hill giant at a distance — except that hill giants do not move like that, with the hunched, stalking patience of a thing that enjoys the chase more than the kill. Her skin is the blue-black of a deep bruise. Her fingers end in iron-hard nails the color of rust, and when she smiles — and she will smile — her teeth are flat, broad, and set in a jaw that could crack a horse’s femur.

name: Aranea
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — dual affinity, with Air dominant
environment: Twin Rivers and Kinsweave (Great Forests, deep canopy; especially common in the Hasura heartlands and the old-growth Deepwood border)
description: It looks like a spider — a hand-sized spider, mottled brown and grey, building a web between two branches with the unhurried patience of a craftsman who has all day. Then you notice the web’s pattern: not random, not instinctive, but geometric — a mandala of concentric shapes that no spider should know how to make. And then it turns, and the cluster of eyes that looks back at you has something behind them that spiders’ eyes do not have.

name: Bakeneko
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — dual affinity, Air dominant
environment: Twin Rivers (Great Forests, Tanuki groves and longhouse settlements; occasionally in Hasura border-villages where forest meets cultivated land)
description: The cat sits on the rooftop of a Tanuki longhouse, washing its face with one paw. It has been sitting there since morning. It was sitting there yesterday, too. The Tanuki children wave to it; the Tanuki elders do not. They have noticed that the cat’s shadow does not match its posture, and that its tail — if you watch long enough — splits at the tip and then mends itself when it realises you are looking.

name: Banshee
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — distorted; the banshee’s wail is wind corrupted by grief
environment: The Kinsweave (border-kingdom ruins, fallen courts, abandoned keeps along the contested frontiers); secondary presence along the Lament Road and in the ruins of the Twelve Kingdoms
description: You hear her before you see her — a sound that begins as a woman weeping, rises to a scream, and then passes beyond screaming into a frequency that lives behind your teeth and in the bones of your skull. The air turns cold. And then she is there, translucent and grey, her mouth open wider than a living woman’s mouth could open, her hair streaming upward as though she were drowning in reverse.

name: Basilisk
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth)
environment: The Infinite Desert (rocky gullies, canyon approaches, ruins with exposed stone foundations); secondary presence in the Glass Wastes (ridgeline scree-fields) and Scorchspires foothills (dry ravines)
description: It moves like a lizard that has never needed to learn haste — a low, heavy-bodied crawler, its scales the grey-brown of sunbaked stone, its eight legs spreading its weight across the sand like a creature designed by someone who did not want it to sink. Its eyes are the problem. They are open, wet, and the colour of old amber, and everything they look at dies — or wishes it had.

name: Behir
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lady of Fire (Fire) — dual affinity; lightning is where wind meets heat
environment: The Infinite Desert (deep canyons, slot canyons, cliff-face lairs between the Sunken Thrones and the Glass Wastes); secondary presence in Giant’s Teeth foothills (steep ravines)
description: The canyon wall moves. What you took for a ridge of dark stone uncoils — twelve legs unfold from its sides, a serpentine body longer than a merchant barge stretches across the gap between the cliff faces, and a head like a crocodile’s opens to reveal a throat lit from within by crackling blue-white light. Lightning arcs between its teeth. The air smells of ozone and hot sand.

name: Black Pudding
flags: elemental_affinity:None — oozes exist outside the elemental framework; see Elemental Null
environment: Underground (Dómnathar tunnel networks, Giant ruin undercrofts, natural cavern systems beneath any region); most commonly encountered in the deep tunnels of the Kinsweave, Gatefall Reach, and beneath the Infinite Desert
description: The tunnel floor is wet. That is not unusual — water seeps through stone in the deep places of the world, collecting in low points, forming pools that reflect lamplight. But this pool does not reflect. It absorbs. The light falls into it and does not come back. And the pool is moving, creeping uphill against the grade, flowing toward the warmth of your torch with a slow, blind hunger.

name: Blink Dog
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air)
environment: The Eternal Steppe (open grasslands, particularly the western steppe where Mytroan herding routes cross Warg territory); secondary presence at the Kinsweave-Steppe border
description: It is there and then it is not. A flicker of gold-brown fur, a flash of bright, intelligent eyes, and the space where the dog was standing is empty — the air closing behind it with a faint pop of displaced pressure. Then it is behind you, wagging its tail, looking at you with an expression that manages to combine the loyalty of a hound with the smugness of a creature that knows you cannot catch it.

name: Bodak
flags: elemental_affinity:None — bodaks are breach-warped; they exist outside the elemental system entirely
environment: Gatefall Reach (Silence Breach zones, dead-Kin-sense areas near Dorrach Kael); secondary presence at the Lament of Kings (unstable resonance zones) and in deep Dómnathar tunnel networks where planar barriers are thin
description: It was alive once — you can tell because it is shaped like something that was alive. Two arms, two legs, a head. But the flesh is the color of burnt tallow, stretched tight over a frame that seems to have been emptied from the inside. The eyes are the worst: pale, featureless white, like two holes punched through the face into a void behind it. It does not breathe. It does not blink. When it looks at you, something behind your sternum goes cold and stops.

name: Bugbear
flags: elemental_affinity:None native — Goblinoid (extraplanar origin, arrived with the Dómnathar host); free bugbears in contact with Oreka’s elemental environment for centuries show trace Earth attunement
alignment: Usually chaotic evil (Deceiver-aligned) or usually neutral (free)
environment: Temperate mountains and forested highlands (Giant’s Teeth foothills, Gatefall Reach ridgelines, Kinsweave hill-country); Deceiver-aligned bugbears are found in Dómnathar tunnel networks and underground strongholds
organization: Solitary, gang (2–4), or warband (5–12 plus 1–3 hobgoblin officers [Deceiver-aligned]) or hunting lodge (5–12 plus 1 elder [free])
description: It is bigger than you expected — half again the height of a man, broad-shouldered, covered in coarse dark fur that smells of wet leather and something else, something sharp and wrong that prickles at the back of your Kin-sense. It moves through the forest with a silence that should be impossible for something its size, and when it looks at you from the underbrush, its yellow eyes hold an intelligence that is not malicious — just utterly, carefully calculating whether you are worth the effort.

name: Bulette
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth)
environment: The Infinite Desert (deep sand-seas, dune-fields between the Sunken Thrones and the Glass Wastes); secondary presence in the Eternal Steppe (deep-soil grasslands, particularly along the Kinsweave border where soil is loosest)
description: The ground heaves. A ridge of displaced earth rises like a wave in the sand — six feet high, forty feet long, moving faster than a horse at gallop. It crests. The sand explodes upward, and something armored and enormous breaches the surface like a whale breaching water: a body the size of a wagon, plated in blue-grey chitin harder than castle stone, with a head that is mostly jaw and a jaw that is mostly teeth. It hangs in the air for one terrible second, blotting out the sun. Then it comes d...

name: Bunyip
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water)
environment: Apelian Sea (coastal shallows, river-mouths, tidal pools, and sheltered coves along the Deepwater Marches coastline); secondary presence at the Sea Falls and in the brackish estuaries where the Great River meets the Apelian basin
description: The water at the edge of the sea-lake goes still — not the stillness of calm water, but the stillness of water holding its breath. Then a shape surfaces: seal-dark, heavy-shouldered, with a head that seems to shift between dog and crocodile depending on the angle. Its mouth opens and it produces a sound — not a roar, not a bark, but something between the two, a bellowing cry that reverberates off the cliff face and turns the blood in your arms to ice water. The fisherfolk are already running....

name: Al Karisi
flags: elemental_affinity:Distorted — the Al Karisi retains a corrupted echo of whatever element it was attuned to in life, but the signature is broken and painful to read
environment: Any settled region where Kin give birth — not bound to a specific territory but drawn to the act of birth itself. Most commonly reported in the Kinsweave border-kingdoms, Tidebloom Reach, and Eruskan river-settlements where births occur in isolated conditions
description: The room smells of blood and birth and then, without warning, of something else — something dry and bitter, like ash mixed with old flowers. The new mother is sleeping, the child beside her. A shape stands in the corner that was not there a moment ago: grey, translucent, wearing the face of a woman who looks almost — but not quite — like someone the midwife once knew. Its hands are long. Its nails are longer. It drifts toward the cradle with the slow, purposeful focus of something that has co...

name: Almas
flags: elemental_affinity:None native (Goblinoid, extraplanar origin); trace Earth attunement developing in steppe-born generations
environment: The Eternal Steppe (deep grasslands, rocky outcrops, the wild margins between Warg territory and Mytroan herding routes)
organization: Solitary, pair, family group (3–6), or band (7–15 plus 1d4 young)
description: It stands at the edge of the Warg camp’s firelight, half-hidden in the long grass — stocky, broad-shouldered, covered head to foot in reddish-brown hair matted with steppe-dust. Its face is flat and heavy-browed, its eyes small and sharp. It carries no weapon, wears no clothing, but there is nothing animal about the way it watches the camp. It is reading the fire. It is counting the Wargs. It is deciding whether tonight is a good night to trade.

name: Amaru
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) primary, Lady of the Sea (Water) secondary — the amaru is a creature of deep stone and mountain water
environment: Giant’s Teeth (high passes, mountain valleys, cliff-face lairs above the treeline); secondary presence along the Orean plateau and in the volcanic foothills where the Giant’s Teeth meet the Scorchspires
description: The mountain pass narrows to a throat of dark stone, and there — coiled across the gap like a living gate — lies something that is neither serpent nor dragon but partakes of both. Its body is scaled in copper and verdigris, the colour shifting with the angle of light like tarnished metal; its head bears two short, curving horns above eyes that glow with a deep amber intelligence. Wings — not bat-leather but feathered, iridescent green-black like a condor’s — lie folded against flanks ribbed w...

name: Amphisbaena
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth)
environment: The Infinite Desert (sandy gullies, rocky scree-fields, ruin-margins, canyon floors); secondary presence in the Glass Wastes and the arid foothills between the Giant’s Teeth and the Scorchspires
description: Something moves in the sand ahead of you — a ripple, like water over a submerged log. Then two heads emerge, one at each end of a body the colour of dried bone, and both heads open their mouths simultaneously, each displaying a set of thin, curved fangs wet with venom. The creature does not coil; it holds itself in a taut S-curve between the two heads, ready to strike in either direction without turning around.

name: Assassin Vine
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) — dual affinity reflecting root-depth and water dependence
environment: Great Forests (Deepwood, Twin Rivers old-growth, dense undergrowth near streams and springs); secondary presence in Tidebloom Reach (tidal forests) and Deepwater Marches (jungle undergrowth)
description: The trail narrows between two stands of old-growth trees, their canopy so thick that the forest floor is dim even at midday. Moss covers everything — the tree trunks, the stones, the trail itself. There is no wind, but something moves at the edge of your vision: a vine, thick as a man’s forearm, sliding across the path with a slow, purposeful motion that has nothing to do with the breeze. By the time you notice that the vines are moving on every side of you, they are already too close.

name: Cadi Kuşu
flags: elemental_affinity:Corrupted — the Cadı Kuşu retains traces of its original Kin affinity, but the signature is cracked and wrong, contaminated by stolen Deceiver magic
environment: Gatefall Reach (settlements near the Silence Breach perimeter, abandoned farmsteads, forest edges); secondary presence in the Kinsweave border-kingdoms and Tidebloom Reach where Dómnathar influence reaches furthest
description: The bird perches on the rooftop at dusk — dark-feathered, crow-sized, with eyes that catch the light wrong. It watches the village with a patience that no animal possesses. When it opens its beak, the sound that emerges is not a birdcall but something like a woman humming through a mouthful of glass. The villagers bar their doors. They know what a Cadı Kuşu is. They know what it used to be.

name: Ccoa
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — dual affinity; the ccoa is where the storm meets the mountain
environment: Giant’s Teeth (high mountain ridgelines, storm-swept passes, exposed plateaus above the treeline); secondary presence along the Scorchspires’ northern ridges where storms gather
description: The storm comes down the mountain wrong. It moves against the wind, coiling between the peaks like something alive. Lightning strikes the same ridgeline three times, four times, and in the flash you see it — a shape in the clouds, sleek and low-slung, with the silhouette of an enormous cat. Its eyes are lightning. Its fur is thunder. It walks across the sky as though the storm were solid ground beneath its feet.

name: Centaur
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) — dual affinity; earth-rooted but wind-spirited
environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the transition zone where the Great Forests thin into grassland, particularly along the Kinsweave-Steppe frontier and the western forest-edge near the Twin Rivers)
organization: Solitary, company (5–8), troop (8–18 plus 1 leader of 2nd–5th level), or herd (20–150 adults plus 30% noncombatants plus 10 3rd-level sergeants plus 5 5th-level lieutenants plus 1 7th-level leader)
description: It comes out of the tree-line at a canter — the lower body of a heavy steppe-horse, the upper body of a broad-chested warrior, carrying a horn-and-sinew composite bow that would take a human archer both hands to string. Its eyes scan the grassland with the alert, unhurried confidence of a creature that has never needed to run from anything. The wind shifts, and you realise there are more of them in the trees — a dozen, perhaps more, watching you with the same calculating patience.

name: Changeling
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — fey affinity aligned with the forest (Wind through branches, Earth in roots)
environment: Great Forests (deep forest, near Kin settlements at the forest edge; also found in Twin Rivers forest-villages and the southern Kinsweave where forest extends into the plains)
organization: Solitary (always when placed in a Kin household; a small clutch of 2–5 may exist in the deep forest in natural form before dispersal)
description: The child in the cradle is wrong. The parents know it. The neighbours know it. The child itself — if it is a child — knows it, and watches the household with eyes that are too old and too amused for its face. It is the right size, the right shape, and it eats when fed, cries when wet, and sleeps when held. But it is not the child that was there yesterday. Something in the forest took the real one and left this in its place.

name: Charun
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of Fire (Fire) — dual affinity; stone shaped by heat, the forge-gate, the threshold between states
environment: Great River (river-gates, bridge-arches, canal locks, and portals along the Great River and its major tributaries — always at a point of transition, where water passes from one state or region to another); most commonly encountered at Eruskan-maintained river-gates
description: The river barge drifts toward the stone gate where the Great River narrows between two carved pillars — remnants of Giant architecture, now maintained by Eruskan hands. Standing on the west bank is a figure that should not be there: tall, heavy-framed, grey-skinned, with a face like weathered stone and eyes like embers in a dead fire. It carries a hammer. Not a weapon — or not only a weapon. A tool. The kind of tool used to shape stone, break locks, and open things that were meant to stay clo...

name: Cherufe
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — dual affinity, Fire dominant; the cherufe is molten earth, the place where the Lady of Fire’s domain and the Lord of Stone’s domain merge
environment: Scorchspires (active calderas, magma chambers, volcanic vent systems, and the deep volcanic core of the Scorchspire mountain range)
description: The caldera glows. Not from below — from across, from the thing that sits in the centre of the lava lake like a man sitting in a bath. It is made of stone. It is on fire. The stone is not burning — the fire is coming from inside, pouring through cracks in a body of black basalt that has the approximate shape of a giant, if a giant were designed by someone who thought “humanoid” was a loose suggestion rather than a requirement. Its eyes are magma. When it moves, the caldera trembles.

name: Chimera
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) dominant, with traces of Lord of Stone (Earth) and Youngest Brother (Air) — a creature of three natures, none fully integrated
environment: Steppe-Mountain Border (the rocky highlands where the Eternal Steppe meets the Giant’s Teeth foothills; secondary presence in the Scorchspire foothills and the dry ridgelines between the Kinsweave and the Infinite Desert)
description: Three heads. That is the first thing you register — three heads on a body that should not be able to support even one of them. The lion’s head roars. The goat’s head screams. The dragon’s head, dark-scaled and smouldering, simply opens its mouth and breathes fire across the rocks where you were standing a moment ago. The body is massive, a fusion of lion and goat with bat-wings spreading wide enough to blot out the pass behind it. It does not look like something that was designed. It looks li...

name: Chuul
flags: elemental_affinity:None — chuuls are alien to Oreka’s elemental framework; see Breach Resonance
environment: Apelian Sea (coastal shallows, tidal caves, submerged ruins along the Apelian basin); secondary presence in Tidebloom Reach (brackish tidal pools, flooded Giant ruins at Hillmeet and Foxfen)
description: It rises from the shallows like a nightmare designed by committee — lobster pincers the size of shields, a segmented carapace slick with brine, and a mass of writhing tentacles where its mouth should be. Its eyes, set on short stalks, swivel independently, tracking two different targets at once. It does not look like it belongs in this water. It does not look like it belongs anywhere. It looks like something that crawled out of a place that has different rules about what shapes are acceptable.

name: Cloaker
flags: elemental_affinity:None — cloakers are alien aberrations; see Breach Resonance
environment: Underground (deep cavern systems, Dómnathar tunnel networks, Giant ruin undercrofts); most commonly encountered beneath the Kinsweave and the Infinite Desert, where deep tunnels connect natural caverns to ancient infrastructure
description: The ceiling is low here, the stone damp, and the shadows beyond the torchlight are thick as wool. You almost walk under it — would have walked under it — except your companion’s torch catches a ripple of movement overhead and something that looked like a hanging sheet of dark leather opens two red eyes and a mouth full of needle-teeth and drops.

name: Cloud Giant
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — overwhelmingly strong, the air-attunement of a creature that was shaped when the Youngest Brother’s power was undivided
environment: Giant’s Teeth (the highest summits above the permanent snowline, cloud-wrapped peaks accessible only by climbing or flight; a handful are known to lair in the upper reaches of the Emberfangs and the northern Ashen Crown)
organization: Solitary, pair (mated), or family (2 adults plus 1d4 young)
description: The summit is not empty. What you took for a formation of ice-covered boulders is a chair — an enormous chair, carved from the living stone of the peak — and sitting in it is a figure thirty feet tall, wrapped in fog as though fog were a garment. Its skin is pale as glacier-melt. Its hair is white and moves in a wind that does not touch you. Its eyes, when it opens them, are the blue of a sky seen from a height where the air runs out.

name: Cockatrice
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — like the basilisk, the cockatrice’s petrification is an expression of Earth dominance
environment: The Eternal Steppe (open grasslands, scrubby hillsides, field-margins near agricultural settlements); secondary presence in the Kinsweave lowlands and along the forest-steppe border
description: It struts out of the tall grass like it owns the steppe — a rooster-sized creature with the body of a scrawny chicken, the wings of a bat, and a tail that ends in a scaly, serpentine point. Its feathers are patchy and iridescent, shifting between sickly green and tarnished bronze. Its eyes are beady, stupid, and mean. It looks ridiculous. Then it pecks a field-mouse and the mouse turns to stone mid-squeak, and you stop laughing.

name: Criosphinx
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — dual affinity reflecting the forge-heat and volcanic stone of the Scorchspires
environment: Scorchspire Foothills (rocky ridgelines, dry valleys, and exposed plateaus at the transition between the Scorchspires’ volcanic slopes and the lowland desert or steppe); secondary presence in the Giant’s Teeth foothills where dry, warm conditions prevail
description: The creature on the ridgeline has the body of a lion — massive, tawny, heavy with muscle — and the head of a ram, curling horns spiralling out from a skull broader than a man’s chest. Its wings are folded against its flanks, eagle-feathered and enormous. It watches you approach with an expression that is neither hostile nor welcoming but evaluative, as though it is calculating something about you that you are not aware of.

name: Cù Sìth
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — the border between wind and root, the place where forest meets grassland
environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the transitional zone where the Great Forests thin into grassland — specifically the Pasua-claimed stretches of the western and southern forest-edge, the Kinsweave-Steppe frontier, and the wooded margins of the Twin Rivers region)
description: The dog is too large. That is the first thing — it stands shoulder-height to a horse, its body lean and deep-chested, built for endurance rather than speed. Its fur is dark green, the colour of moss on old stone, and it moves through the undergrowth without disturbing a single branch. Its eyes are gold and ancient and watching you with an intelligence that no dog should possess. It does not bark. It does not growl. It simply appears at the tree-line, considers you, and waits to see what you w...

name: Culsans
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lady of the Sea (Water) — dual affinity; the culsans embodies the meeting of currents, the point where rivers converge and winds cross
environment: Great River (river-forks, confluence points, canal junctions, and crossroads along the Great River and its major tributaries — always at a point where two paths, two currents, or two directions meet); most commonly encountered at Eruskan-maintained river-forks in the Twin Rivers region
description: The gate stands at the river-fork where the Twin Rivers meet — two pillars of Giant-cut stone, the carvings worn smooth by centuries of water and wind. Between the pillars, a figure stands facing both directions at once. It has two faces — one looking upstream, one looking downstream — set on a single head that rotates slowly, continuously, like the hands of a clock that measures something other than time. Its body is carved from the same stone as the gate, or perhaps the gate was carved from...

name: Darkmantle
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — faint, ambient; the darkmantle is a cave creature through and through
environment: Underground (natural cavern systems, Giant ruin undercrofts, Dómnathar tunnel fringes); found beneath any region with cave networks, most commonly reported in Vestri mining territories and the tunnels beneath the Infinite Desert
description: The stalactite opens its eyes. That is what it looks like — a cone of wet stone hanging from the cavern ceiling suddenly sprouts two tiny, reflective eyes and unfurls a membrane of leathery skin that was folded so tightly against its body that it was invisible. Then it drops.

name: Delver
flags: elemental_affinity:None — delvers are alien aberrations; see Breach Resonance
environment: Deep Underground (beneath any region; most commonly encountered in the deepest strata beneath the Infinite Desert and Giant’s Teeth, where natural cave systems intersect alien geology)
description: The wall is moving. Not collapsing — moving, deliberately, as though the stone were being dissolved from within. Something vast and pale presses through the rock face, its body slick with the acid that eats the tunnel open ahead of it. It is the size of a house. It is the shape of a slug designed by something that thought in terms of bore-diameters and load-bearing capacities. Its hide glistens. The stone hisses and smokes where it passes.

name: Derro
flags: elemental_affinity:None — derro are alien to Oreka’s elemental system; see Breach Resonance
environment: Deep Underground (natural cavern systems beneath any region, particularly the deep strata beneath the Kinsweave and Giant’s Teeth where derro warrens intersect Vestri mining tunnels)
organization: Team (2–4), squad (5–8 plus 1 3rd-level sorcerer), or band (11–20 plus 30% noncombatants plus 3 3rd-level sorcerers plus 1 5th-level savant leader)
description: Small. Blue-skinned. Mad. Those are the three things every Vestri miner knows about derro, and they are listed in order of importance. Small enough to fit through cracks you wouldn’t think twice about. Blue-skinned, the colour of deep-water bruise, which means you can lose them in torchlight shadow. Mad, which means they will do things no sane creature would do — attack a party of armed dwarves with stone knives, set traps in tunnels they themselves use, experiment on captured prisoners with ...

name: Domovoi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — fire dominant; the domovoi is a hearth-spirit, born where fire meets stone
environment: Scorchspires (Rarozhki dwarf forges, hearthstones, and workshops — always inside a structure with an active fire source); rare secondary presence in Eruskan river-settlements and Pekakarlik oasis-forges
description: The forge-fire is banked for the night, the embers glowing low, and something moves in the warm space between the anvil and the hearthstone. It is small — no taller than a dwarf-child — and shaped like a very old dwarf who has been compressed by time until everything non-essential has been squeezed out, leaving only beard, eyes, and an expression of severe domestic judgment. It checks the fire. It checks the tools. It picks up a hammer that has been left on the wrong hook and places it on the...

name: Doppelganger
flags: elemental_affinity:None native — doppelgangers are alien to Oreka; see Breach Resonance
environment: Any settlement (doppelgangers are urban predators found wherever Kin populations are dense enough to provide cover — primarily Kinsweave border-kingdoms, Eruskan river-cities, Tidebloom Reach towns, and the larger Pekakarlik oasis-settlements)
description: It looks like you. It looks exactly like you — your face, your build, your clothes (not real, just shaped flesh mimicking fabric), and the way you hold your shoulders when you are tired. It even smells like you, or close enough that a casual observer would not notice. But its thoughts are not your thoughts, and behind the mask of your face, something alien is calculating how long it can wear you before someone notices.

name: Dragon Turtle
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lady of Fire (Fire) — dual affinity; the dragon turtle is the Lady of the Sea’s domain armoured in the Lady of Fire’s heat
environment: Kailian Sea (deep channels, submerged seamounts, and the warmer currents of the eastern Kailian basin); secondary presence in the deep Apelian Sea (rare) and at the Great River’s mouth where freshwater meets salt
description: The sea ahead goes flat. Not calm — flat, as though the water itself has been pressed down by something enormous beneath the surface. Then the shell rises: a ridge of stone and coral the size of a small island, streaming water, trailing kelp gardens that have been growing on its back for decades. The head emerges last — a dragon’s head on a turtle’s neck, armoured in scales the colour of deep ocean, with eyes like amber lanterns. The mouth opens. Steam pours out. The fishing boats turn and run.

name: Dragonne
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — dual affinity; the dragonne is a warm-stone predator, fire in a lion’s body
environment: Warm Mountains and Foothills (Scorchspire foothills, southern Giant’s Teeth, dry ridgelines between the Infinite Desert and the Eternal Steppe)
description: It crouches on the ridgeline like something that could not decide whether to be a lion or a dragon and chose both. Its body is a lion’s — massive, tawny, thick with muscle — but its head is elongated, half-snouted, with brass-coloured scales running from brow to shoulders. Enormous bat-leather wings fold along its flanks, and when it opens its mouth to roar, the sound that comes out is not a lion’s roar and not a dragon’s bellow but something between: a brassy, resonant blast that shakes the ...

name: Draugr
flags: elemental_affinity:Corrupted — the draugr carries a distorted echo of the Vestri Earth/Fire resonance, broken by death and further warped by the Dómnathar raising that failed to hold
environment: Tidebloom Reach and northern Kinsweave (barrow-mounds, battlefield graves, and ruined fortifications along the route the Deceiver host marched from Hillmeet to Aldenheim — the “Dead Road”); secondary presence in the Lament of Kings perimeter
description: The barrow-mound splits open. It has not been opened by tools — the stone capstone has been pushed aside from within, scraped along the ground by hands that are still strong after three hundred years. What climbs out is wearing the remains of Vestri plate-armour — Mithral Legion insignia still visible beneath the corrosion, the breastplate cracked where the killing blow landed. Its eyes glow the dull orange of banked forge-coals. Its beard, once braided in the Vestri warrior tradition, hangs ...

name: Dread Wraith
flags: elemental_affinity:None — a dread wraith is elemental negation, a wound in the fabric where resonance used to exist
environment: Any (found wherever sufficient death-energy has accumulated — the Lament of Kings, major battlefields of the Deceivers’ War, the ruins of Hillmeet and Foxfen, and deep Giant tombs where the Thalorien’s grief pooled into darkness)
organization: Solitary or gang (2–5 wraiths led by 1 dread wraith)
description: It comes through the wall without opening the wall. One moment the corridor is empty; the next, a shape of absolute darkness occupies the space where the torchlight ends — not shadow, because shadow is the absence of light, and this is the presence of something else. It is cold the way a blade is sharp: not a quality of the air but a quality of the thing itself, radiating outward, turning breath to frost at thirty feet. Its face — if the darkness where a face should be can be called a face — ...

name: Dryad
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) — root and sap; the dryad is bound to the land and the water that sustains it
environment: Great Forests (old-growth oak groves, ancient forest, anywhere a single tree has stood long enough to develop fey resonance — primarily Hasura territory, Twin Rivers old-growth, and the deeper Pasua forest-margins)
description: She is standing inside the oak. Not behind it — inside it, her body half-merged with the bark, her skin the colour of heartwood, her hair a cascade of leaves that shift with a breeze that does not touch any other tree. Her eyes are green. Not metaphorically — green the way chlorophyll is green, the green of photosynthesis, the green of something that feeds on light. She watches you with an expression that is not human curiosity. It is the expression of a tree noticing that something is standi...

name: Dullahan
flags: elemental_affinity:Corrupted — the dullahan carries a twisted echo of the Far Rider’s air-earth resonance (wind-over-grassland), broken by death and scarred by Dómnathar raising
environment: Great Forests and Eternal Steppe (the Dead Road and the old Far Rider patrol routes that stretch from the forest-steppe border through the Tidebloom Reach to the edges of the Lament of Kings; occasionally found in deep Pasua territory where Far Rider outposts once stood)
organization: Solitary or patrol (2–4 dullahan riding together)
description: The rider comes down the Dead Road at a gallop, and the horse beneath it is wrong — too thin, too dark, its hooves striking sparks that glow the colour of old blood. The rider sits tall in the saddle, armoured in the remnants of Far Rider scout-leather, a sword in one hand and nothing on its shoulders. Nothing. The head is gone. Where the neck ends, a ragged stump of old flesh and exposed bone meets the open air, and the air around the stump shimmers with a faint, sick light that is neither f...

name: Dust Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — minor elemental; dust is where wind meets dry earth
environment: The Infinite Desert (sandstorms, dust-devils, ruin interiors, dry canyon systems); secondary presence wherever arid conditions meet high winds (Scorchspire valleys, Glass Wastes, Ashen Crown leeward slopes)
description: It materialises from the air itself — or rather, from the dust in the air, drawing particles together into a shape roughly the size and temperament of an angry cat with wings. Its body is gritty, compacted sand and dust that shifts and hisses when it moves. Its eyes are two sparks of dull amber. Its voice, when it speaks (and it will speak — dust mephits never shut up), sounds like sand pouring through a crack.

name: Dybbuk
flags: elemental_affinity:None — goblinoid dead carry the same absence of elemental resonance as living goblinoids, but twisted by undeath into something colder
environment: Any area with goblinoid populations (Deepwater Marches free goblin settlements, Eternal Steppe Warg communities that harbour goblins, Dómnathar tunnel fringes where goblin auxiliaries are stationed)
description: The goblin stops mid-sentence. Its companions notice the silence first — Krikkit never stops talking, has not stopped talking since the day it learned Common from a halfling trader — and then they notice the eyes. Krikkit’s eyes have gone flat. Not dead. Not vacant. Full, the way a cup is full when someone has poured something different into it. The goblin’s mouth opens and a voice comes out that is not Krikkit’s voice. It is older, heavier, and it speaks Goblin with an accent that hasn’t bee...

name: Dire Ape
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth/Air (canopy and trunk — the Deepwood Paragon Ape is an expression of forest verticality)
environment: Deepwood Canopy (the highest, densest sections of the Great Forests, particularly the southern Deepwood where the canopy forms an unbroken ceiling 100+ feet above the forest floor)
organization: Solitary or company (2–4, siblings from the same Paragon siring)
description: Large Animal

name: Dire Bear
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth (the mountain-and-forest bear, stone-dense and rooted)
environment: Giant’s Teeth Foothills (forested mountain slopes, river valleys in the lower Giant’s Teeth, the wooded approaches to Orean plateau settlements)
description: Large Animal

name: Dire Boar
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth (the rooter, the ground-breaker — dire boars are earth made aggressive)
environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the transition zone between the Great Forests and the Eternal Steppe, where undergrowth and grassland overlap)
organization: Solitary or herd (2–4 with mundane boars)
description: Large Animal

name: Dire Lion
flags: elemental_affinity:Fire/Earth (the sun-hunter — dire lions carry the Lady of Fire’s warmth in their blood)
environment: Eternal Steppe (the deep grasslands far from settlements, particularly the central steppe where the grass grows shoulder-high and the horizon is unbroken in every direction)
description: Large Animal

name: Dire Tiger
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth/Water (the Deepwood hunter — shadow, patience, and the water-courses that thread the forest floor)
environment: Deepwood (the densest interior of the Great Forests, along river-courses and near forest pools where large prey comes to drink)
description: Large Animal

name: Dire Wolf
flags: elemental_affinity:Air/Earth (the wind-runner — dire wolves carry the steppe wind in their stride)
environment: Forests and Eternal Steppe (the forest-steppe border, deep woodlands, and the open grasslands — dire wolves range more widely than any other dire animal)
organization: Solitary, pair, or pack (1 dire wolf leading 5–8 normal wolves)
description: Large Animal

name: Each-Uisge
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — deep, cold, and patient; the Each-Uisge is the Lady’s domain at its most predatory
environment: Great Forests (deep forest pools, still lakes, rain-fed tarns surrounded by old-growth trees — specifically the deep interior of Pasua territory, where standing water collects in ancient glacial depressions beneath the canopy)
description: The horse stands at the lake’s edge, knee-deep in black water, white as birch-bark and perfectly still. It is the most beautiful horse you have ever seen — long-necked, deep-chested, with a mane that streams in a breeze you cannot feel. Its eyes are dark and liquid and fixed on you with an intensity that is not equine. The water around its legs does not ripple. It waits, patient as a lure, for you to come close enough to touch it. Every Pasua child knows what happens next. You touch it. Your ...

name: Earth Elemental, Small
description: The ground buckles. A section of stone floor — ten feet across, solid granite — rises, cracks, and reshapes itself into something upright, something that has arms and legs and a suggestion of a face carved into what might be a head. It smells of deep stone. It sounds like a quarry. When it moves, the ground shakes.

name: Earth Elemental, Medium
description: The ground buckles. A section of stone floor — ten feet across, solid granite — rises, cracks, and reshapes itself into something upright, something that has arms and legs and a suggestion of a face carved into what might be a head. It smells of deep stone. It sounds like a quarry. When it moves, the ground shakes.

name: Earth Elemental, Large
description: The ground buckles. A section of stone floor — ten feet across, solid granite — rises, cracks, and reshapes itself into something upright, something that has arms and legs and a suggestion of a face carved into what might be a head. It smells of deep stone. It sounds like a quarry. When it moves, the ground shakes.

name: Earth Elemental, Huge
description: The ground buckles. A section of stone floor — ten feet across, solid granite — rises, cracks, and reshapes itself into something upright, something that has arms and legs and a suggestion of a face carved into what might be a head. It smells of deep stone. It sounds like a quarry. When it moves, the ground shakes.

name: Ember Drake
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) — pure, undiluted fire-affinity; the ember drake is the Lady’s domain in its most accessible form
environment: Scorchspires (volcanic vents, lava tubes, caldera edges, and Rarozhki forge-halls); secondary presence in the Emberfang Mountains, Ashen Crown, and volcanic vents in the Infinite Desert
advancement: 5–8 HD (Small); see Elder Drake and Emberfang Wyrm for mature forms
description: It sits on the forge-rim like a cat sits on a warm hearth — compact, content, and radiating heat that makes the air shimmer. It is the size of a large dog, scaled in overlapping plates of orange-red that shift to white-hot at the edges, and its eyes glow like coals. A thin trail of smoke curls from each nostril. The Rarozhki smith working at the forge does not look up. The drake has been here longer than he has. It was here when his grandfather lit this forge for the first time, and it will b...

name: Encantado
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — the encantado is the Lady’s domain expressed as charm rather than force
environment: Apelian Sea and Deepwater Marches coast (warm coastal shallows, river-mouths, tidal lagoons; in dolphin form, ranges across the full Apelian basin; in humanoid form, visits coastal settlements, particularly halfling fishing villages and the smaller Deepwater Marches trading-posts)
description: The stranger walks out of the sea at dusk, barefoot on the wet sand, wearing a hat pulled low over their forehead. They are beautiful — unnaturally, painfully beautiful — and they move with a liquid grace that makes every step look like swimming. They smile at the fishing village and the fishing village smiles back, because it has been a long time since anyone beautiful came to these shores. The hat stays on. It always stays on. Beneath the hat, the blowhole is still wet.

name: Ettercap
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — faint; the ettercap is a creature of the under-canopy, the dark spaces close to the ground
environment: Great Forests (dense mid-canopy and undergrowth, particularly in humid lowland forest where spider populations are highest — southern Deepwood, Tidebloom Reach tidal forests, and the jungle-margins of the Deepwater Marches)
organization: Solitary, pair, or nest (3–6 plus 2–8 monstrous spiders)
description: The web is wrong. Normal spider-webs are symmetrical, elegant, efficient — this one is a sprawling mess of thick, ropy strands strung between trees at head-height, and it is not empty. Something crouches in the upper canopy where the anchor-lines converge: humanoid enough to have arms, legs, and a recognisable posture, but wrong in the details — too many joints in the fingers, spinnerets where a chin should be, and eyes that are flat, black, and arranged in a cluster rather than a pair.

name: Ettin
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — fragmented, faint; the ettin carries the residual earth-resonance of the Giant heritage, corrupted by degeneration into something dull and heavy
environment: Giant’s Teeth (mountain caves, rocky overhangs, and abandoned Giant ruin-margins in the lower peaks; occasionally found in the Scorchspire foothills and the dry badlands between the Giant’s Teeth and the Infinite Desert)
organization: Solitary, pair, gang (3–4), troupe (1–2 plus 1–2 brown bears), or band (3–5 plus 1–2 brown bears)
description: Two heads argue over which way to go. This is what you hear first — the voices, deep and guttural, snapping at each other in a language that is almost Giant but not quite, the way a parrot’s speech is almost language but not quite. Then the owner of the voices steps around the boulder and you see both heads on a body that is thirteen feet tall, hunched, broad as a cart, and carrying a morningstar in each hand. Both heads notice you simultaneously. They stop arguing. For the first time in minu...

name: Girallon
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) — the canopy and the trunk; girallons are creatures of the vertical forest
environment: Deepwood (the densest interior of the Great Forests — specifically the mid-to-upper canopy zone, 60–120 feet above the forest floor, where the branches are thick enough to support their weight and the undergrowth is too dense for ground-travel)
description: Four arms. That is what you notice first — not the size (though it is massive, nine feet at the shoulder), not the white fur (though it is matted and streaked with old blood), not the fangs (though they are longer than your fingers). Four arms, each one thick as a man’s thigh, each one ending in a hand that could crush a helmet like an egg. The creature drops from the canopy on four of those arms and lands in front of you on two legs, and the remaining four spread wide, and every hand is open...

name: Gnoll
flags: elemental_affinity:None — gnolls exist outside the elemental framework, like all non-Kin peoples
environment: Steppe Margins (the dry, broken terrain between the Eternal Steppe and the foothills of the Giant’s Teeth and Scorchspires — badlands, scrubland, and seasonal water-courses)
organization: Solitary, pair, hunting party (2–5 plus 1–2 hyenas), or band (10–100 plus 50% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1 leader of 4th–6th level plus 5–8 hyenas)
description: The hyena-headed warrior watches you from the ridge with an intelligence that has nothing to do with its animal face. It wears armour — not well-made, not beautiful, but functional, scavenged and resized and maintained. It holds a flail in one hand with the ease of long practice. Behind it, on the reverse slope, you can hear others — three, five, maybe more — and the yipping laughter that is either communication or amusement or both.

name: Goblin
flags: elemental_affinity:None — goblinoids exist outside the elemental framework
environment: Any (goblins are the most widespread non-Kin people in Oreka — found in every region, in every terrain, in every conceivable ecological niche from Deepwood caves to desert ruins to mountain tunnels to steppe-margin camps)
organization: Gang (4–9), band (10–100 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1 leader of 4th–6th level), warband (10–24 with worg mounts), or settlement (40–400 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1–2 lieutenants of 4th–5th level plus 1 leader of 6th–8th level plus 10–24 worg mounts plus 2–4 dire wolves)
description: It is three feet tall, green-skinned, loud, and there are always more of them than you expected. The one crouching behind the rock has a knife. The one in the tree has a shortbow. The one you did not see has just stolen your waterskin. Goblins are not dangerous individually — a single goblin against a single armed Kin is a mismatch that ends quickly. But goblins are never single, never alone, and never out of ideas.

name: Gorgon
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of Fire (Fire) — the gorgon is metal incarnate, the place where stone meets fire and becomes iron
environment: Eternal Steppe (open grasslands, particularly the dry central steppe where iron-rich earth supports sparse, tough vegetation; secondary presence in the Scorchspire foothills where volcanic soils contain high metal concentrations)
description: The iron bull stands in the grassland and the grassland is afraid of it. That is the only way to describe what you see: the grass bends away from it, the insects avoid it, the air around it tastes of metal filings. It is the size of a large ox, armoured in overlapping plates of dark iron that are not armour — they are its hide, grown from its flesh, as much a part of it as your skin is part of you. It lowers its head. Steam rises from its nostrils. The steam smells like a forge on a bad day.

name: Gray Ooze
flags: elemental_affinity:None — oozes exist outside the elemental framework
environment: Underground (any subterranean environment — natural caves, mine-tunnels, Giant-era passages, Dómnathar corridors; most common in wet underground environments where runoff collects)
description: The stone floor is wet. Not water-wet — acid-wet, the kind of wet that dissolves boot-leather in seconds and eats through chainmail in minutes. The puddle on the floor is not a puddle. It is three inches deep, six feet across, and moving toward you with the purposeful slowness of something that has never needed to hurry because nothing it touches survives long enough to escape.

name: Green Hag
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — corrupted; the green hag is the Lady’s domain gone stagnant and poisonous
environment: Tidebloom Marshes (the tidal marshlands of the Tidebloom Reach, particularly the deep-marsh zones where freshwater rivers meet saltwater tidal intrusions and the water is neither one thing nor the other)
organization: Solitary or covey (3 hags of any kind plus other creatures)
description: She lives where the water goes bad. Not where the water is dangerous — that is different, honest danger, the kind you can see. She lives where the water is wrong: where the marsh-pools turn the wrong colour, where the fish float belly-up without visible cause, where the air tastes of sweetness that your body tells you means rot. She is there, somewhere in the murk, watching you with yellow eyes that she has not had to disguise in decades because no one comes here anymore.

name: Grick
flags: elemental_affinity:None — aberration; breach resonance indicates origin outside Oreka’s elemental framework
environment: Underground (natural cave systems, mine-tunnels, and Giant-era passages — anywhere with consistent darkness, moisture, and sufficient prey)
description: The tunnel narrows. The walls are wet. Something moves in the darkness ahead — not quickly, not dramatically, but with the patient, questing motion of a creature that has all the time in the world and knows that everything in the tunnel eventually comes to it. You see tentacles first — four of them, barbed, extending from the darkness like fingers testing the air. Then the beak.

name: Griffon
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — the griffon is sky-and-mountain, the Youngest Brother’s wind beneath the Lord of Stone’s weight
environment: Giant’s Teeth Foothills and temperate highlands (rocky hills and upland meadows with clear sight-lines, nesting on cliff-faces and high ledges — the transition zone between the Giant’s Teeth mountains and the lower grasslands)
description: The shadow passes over you first — wide, fast, and shaped wrong for a bird. You look up and see something impossible: a lion’s body, golden and muscular, suspended beneath an eagle’s wings that span thirty feet. The front half is pure raptor — hooked beak, taloned forelegs, golden feathers merging seamlessly into tawny fur at the shoulders. It banks, catches the thermal rising from the hillside, and circles once, looking down at you with a golden eagle’s eye that misses nothing.

name: Grig
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) — the grig is the playful overlap of earth and air; the cricket in the grass, the music on the breeze
environment: Great Forests (forest clearings, meadow-margins, stream-banks, and the sun-dappled edges of the canopy — wherever the forest is light and warm enough for music)
description: You hear the fiddle first. Something is playing a fiddle in the undergrowth — playing it well, playing it fast, playing a reel that makes your feet want to move before your brain can object. You part the ferns and see the musician: a creature the size of your hand, cricket-legged, fairy-winged, grinning like a lunatic, sawing away at a fiddle the size of a matchstick with a bow the size of a needle. It sees you seeing it and plays faster.

name: Grimlock
flags: elemental_affinity:None — grimlocks exist outside the elemental framework, like all non-Kin peoples
environment: Deep Underground (natural cave systems below the depth of Vestri mining, below Dómnathar corridors, in the deep strata where light has never reached and the concept of “surface” is an abstraction)
organization: Gang (2–4), pack (10–20), or tribe (20–40 plus 1–3 leaders of 3rd–5th level plus 2–5 dire bats)
description: It has no eyes. That is the first thing you notice — the flat, smooth expanse of face where eyes should be, unbroken by sockets, lids, or any concession to sight. It does not need eyes. It navigates by sound, by scent, by the vibration of your footsteps through the stone, and by a sense you cannot name that tells it exactly where you are, how large you are, and where to put the axe.

name: Grootslang
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — primordial; the grootslang’s affinity is not the refined, balanced expression of later elemental creatures but raw, undivided fire-and-earth from before the Elemental Lords distinguished between their domains
environment: Volcanic Caves (the deep volcanic tube-systems of the Scorchspires and Emberfang Mountains — not surface calderas but the interior passages where magma flows beneath the mountain-skin; specifically the caves that Taraf-Imro traditions mark as sacred and forbidden)
description: The cave breathes. That is not metaphor — the air moves in and out of the volcanic tube in a rhythm that predates language, and the rhythm is the breathing of the thing that lives inside. It has lived there since before the Giants came. Since before the Kin were shaped. Since before the Elemental Lords withdrew from the world and left their domains to spin on without them. It is the size of a building — an elephant’s body fused with a serpent’s tail, scaled in obsidian, radiating heat that ma...

name: Gynosphinx
flags: elemental_affinity:All Four Elemental Lords (fragmented) — the gynosphinx carries the same four-element signature as the vanished Giants, but refracted, partial, and distorted through a bestial lens
environment: Glass Wastes (the heat-fused sand expanses of the Infinite Desert’s western margins, where the sand has been melted into glass by ancient elemental forces — a landscape of razor-edged glass ridges and reflective plains that the sphinxes have claimed as their own)
description: She is waiting for you. She has been waiting for you since before you entered the desert, since before you decided to take this route, since before you were born. She reclines on the sand-polished stone with the patience of a monument and the attention of a predator, and her face — a woman’s face, beautiful, ageless, and utterly without mercy — watches you approach with an expression that might be curiosity or might be appetite. They are the same thing, to her.

name: Hakuturi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) — the living forest; root-water and dark soil, growth expressed as patience
environment: Great Forests (the old-growth stands of the Kovaka territories — the southern and island-fringe forests where ancient trees have stood for millennia undisturbed; never found in young growth, managed forest, or areas cleared within living memory)
description: The tree is old. Older than the Kovaka settlement at its base, older than the trail that leads to it, older than anything human memory can account for. You came to cut a branch — a single branch, for a canoe-rib — and now you cannot lift the axe. Not because something is stopping you. Because the tree is looking at you. The bark has rearranged itself into a face that was always there and always watching, and the face is not angry. It is patient. It is waiting for you to understand why you sho...

name: Harpy
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — corrupted; the harpy is the wind made cruel, the Youngest Brother’s domain twisted into a lure
environment: Cliffs and Marshes (coastal cliffs along the Apelian Sea, the marshy fringes of the Tidebloom Reach, and the ruin-studded cliffsides of the Kinsweave — anywhere that combines elevation with nearby prey-populations)
description: The singing starts before you see her. It comes from above — from the cliff-face, from the marsh-edge, from the ruin’s upper story — and it is beautiful. Not just pleasant. Beautiful in the way that makes you stop walking, stop thinking, stop being afraid. Your legs move toward the sound before your brain can object. By the time you see what is singing — the matted feathers, the human face twisted by cruelty, the taloned feet — you are already too close.

name: Hieracosphinx
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — faint; the hieracosphinx carries a diluted version of the four-element sphinx resonance, stripped to a single attenuated air-note
environment: Infinite Desert (the Glass Wastes and surrounding dune-seas — the same general region as gynosphinxes, but occupying the less desirable territories that the greater sphinxes do not claim)
description: It has a hawk’s head and a lion’s body and none of the intelligence of its gynosphinx cousins. It does not pose riddles. It does not bargain. It sits on a sand-scoured ridge in the Glass Wastes and watches the desert below with eyes that calculate nothing except distance, angle, and speed.

name: Goreham (Brute)
flags: elemental_affinity:None — artificial earth-attunement, cosmetic only
environment: Temperate Hills (the low foothills and rolling terrain between the Giant’s Teeth and the settled lowlands — lower elevation than Hrokk territory, further from mountain peaks than Fogwrack lairs, occupying the most accessible terrain where Brutes can sustain themselves on livestock, game, and stolen food)
organization: Solitary, pair, gang (3–4), or mob (5–8, rare — a degraded War-era formation, less cohesive than Hrokk warbands)
description: It is ten feet tall and most of that is gut. Where the Fogwrack is a mockery of Giant grace and the Hrokk is a mockery of Giant efficiency, the Goreham is a mockery of Giant endurance — a lumbering, thick-skinned, dim-witted slab of Dómnathar-manufactured flesh that exists to absorb damage and keep moving forward. It is not fast. It is not clever. It is not frightening in the way the Fogwrack is frightening. It is simply, relentlessly, stupidly present, and it will not stop coming until it is...

name: Hippogriff
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — similar to griffons but lighter, more air-dominant; the hippogriff is wind-over-hill rather than wind-over-mountain
environment: Giant’s Teeth Foothills (the low, grassy hills and upland meadows of the Giant’s Teeth margins — lower elevation than griffon territory, in terrain where grassland meets slope and thermal-updrafts are strong)
description: The horse has wings. That is the simplest, most reductive description, and it is wrong — the creature is not a horse with wings any more than a griffon is an eagle with a lion’s body. It is its own thing: part raptor, part equine, shaped by the same elemental forces that produced griffons but with a different emphasis. Where the griffon is all predator, the hippogriff is half-wild, half-willing, and that willingness is what makes it valuable.

name: Huallallo
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — primordial; like the grootslang, the huallallo’s affinity is raw and undivided, from an age when fire and earth were not yet separate concepts
environment: Giant’s Teeth Volcanoes (the active and semi-active volcanic peaks in the southern Giant’s Teeth, where the mountain range meets the Scorchspires — specifically the calderas and lava-tube systems that Orean mythology identifies as “the Lady’s Anger”)
description: The mountain is angry. That is what the Orean herders say when the ground shakes and the sky turns orange and the air tastes of sulphur thick enough to chew. The mountain is angry, and the anger has a shape. It walks out of the caldera on legs of cooling basalt, trailing magma behind it like a cloak, and it is thirty feet tall and burning from the inside. Its eyes are white-hot. Its hands are fists of volcanic glass. The Orean do not run from it — you cannot outrun a volcanic eruption. They p...

name: Huldra
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — the forest-river border; where water meets earth and both are alive
environment: Forest-River Borders (the banks and shallows of rivers that flow through the Great Forests — specifically the transition zone where forest canopy overhangs running water, and the worlds of wood and water overlap)
description: She is standing in the river. The water is waist-deep and moving fast, and she is standing in it as though it were a warm bath — barefoot on the stones, her back to you, her long hair trailing in the current. She is beautiful. You can tell that even from behind, from the line of her shoulders and the way the water parts around her as though it likes her. Then she turns, and you see her face, and she is more beautiful than you expected. And then you see her back — or rather, you see that her b...

name: Humbaba
flags: elemental_affinity:All Four Elemental Lords — integrated; the humbaba was built with the Firstborn’s four-element mastery, its elemental core containing bound fragments of all four domains
environment: Giant Ruins (the interior of the best-preserved Giant-era structures — specifically guarding doorways, halls, treasure-vaults, and sacred chambers; found in ruins across the Infinite Desert, Giant’s Teeth, and Kinsweave deep-sites)
organization: Solitary or pair (one per doorway, sometimes two flanking a major entrance)
description: The door opens and the guardian is there. It has been there for seven thousand years. It is fifteen feet tall, built of stone and bronze and elemental fire bound into a framework that the Giants designed to last forever, and it has. Its face is a lion’s face carved in basalt. Its body is a humanoid chassis of interlocking stone plates over a skeleton of Giant-forged bronze. Its eyes — two points of white-hot elemental fire — track you from the doorway to the threshold, and the voice that emer...

name: Hydra
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — hydras are creatures of the deep marsh, saturated with stagnant water-energy
environment: Marshes (the Tidebloom Reach, the Deepwater Marches wetlands, and the marshy lowlands where the Great River spreads into seasonal flood-plains — any warm, wet, low-lying terrain with sufficient prey)
advancement: 6–7 HD (five heads, Huge); 8–10 HD (six to seven heads, Huge); 11–14 HD (eight to ten heads, Gargantuan)
description: Five heads. Five necks. One body. The body is the size of a horse and scaled in dark, wet green; the necks are each ten feet long and independently mobile, weaving and striking like snakes operated by a single furious brain. When one head lunges, the others cover it. When one head is hurt, the others strike at the attacker. The creature does not think — five heads do not produce five times the intelligence, they produce five times the aggression.

name: Impundulu
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lady of Fire (Fire) — lightning; the impundulu is the storm made predatory, the point where wind becomes fire
environment: Deep Steppe (the open grasslands of the Eternal Steppe’s interior, far from mountains or forest — flat terrain where storms build without obstruction and the impundulu can hunt unimpeded by canopy or cliff)
description: The storm comes from nowhere. The sky was clear, the wind was calm, and now the air tastes of copper and the hair on your arms stands straight. Lightning strikes the ground fifty feet away — not from a cloud but from a bird. It is enormous, black-feathered, white-winged, riding the static charge it generates like a ship rides a wave. Its eyes are blue-white and crackling. When it screams, the sound is thunder.

name: Indrik
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — ancient; the indrik is younger than the grootslang and huallallo but still immensely old, a creature from the age when the Elemental Lords’ domains were raw but beginning to differentiate
environment: Scorchspire Interior (the deep volcanic valleys and caldera-basins of the Scorchspire mountain range’s interior — terrain too hostile for permanent Kin habitation, accessible only through passes that the Rarozhki have sealed and marked as sacred)
description: The mountain is supposed to be empty. The Rarozhki sealed this valley three generations ago — declared it sacred, marked the approaches, and turned back every expedition. You came anyway, because the things people seal away are usually valuable. Now you understand why they sealed it. The creature standing in the valley-floor is the size of a house — a massive, four-legged beast with a single spiralling horn of black obsidian rising from its skull, its body armoured in volcanic stone plates th...

name: Inkanyamba
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Youngest Brother (Air) — storm-water; the inkanyamba commands the intersection of water and wind, generating weather through elemental will
environment: Inland Lakes (the large, deep freshwater lakes of the Taraf-Imro territories in the southern mountains and volcanic lowlands — specifically the caldera-lakes and tectonic rift-lakes that are deep enough to support a Huge aquatic predator year-round)
description: The lake has been wrong for three days. The fishermen noticed first — the water-level rising when it should be falling, the currents running backward, the fish moving to the shallows in panicked schools. Then the storms started. Not regular storms — targeted storms, centred on the lake, rotating with a focus that suggests intelligence rather than weather. Something beneath the surface is angry, and whatever it is, it is large enough to control the water above it.

name: Jorogumo
flags: elemental_affinity:None — aberration; breach resonance, deliberately masked
environment: Gatefall Reach (the waterfall-basins, pool-edges, and cliff-hollows of the Gatefall — the great chain of waterfalls in Hasura territory where the river-systems descend from the highland forests to the lowland valleys; specifically the remote, infrequently-patrolled falls where prey can be taken without immediate discovery)
advancement: 8–10 HD (Medium); 11–14 HD (Large — true spider form only)
description: She is beautiful. She is always beautiful — that is the point, that is the design, that is the trap. She sits at the edge of the Gatefall, where the forest canopy overhangs the waterfall-pool, and she combs her hair with her fingers and watches the water and waits for someone to find her. The Hasura scouts found the cocoons first — three of them, wrapped in silk the colour of moonlight, hanging from the underside of the cliff-ledge behind the falls. Inside each cocoon, a Hasura elf. Two were ...

name: Kappa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — kappa are water-spirits given physical, mischievous form
environment: Apelian Shoals (the shallow coastal waters, tidal pools, and estuaries of the Apelian Sea — particularly the sheltered coves and reef-shallows along the Pekakarlik and Na’wasua coast)
description: The thing in the shallows looks like a turtle that decided to be a child. It is three feet tall, green-skinned, shell-backed, with a beak where its mouth should be and a shallow depression in the top of its skull filled with water. It is watching you from behind a rock with an expression that combines curiosity, malice, and hunger in roughly equal measure. The Pekakarlik fisherman who warned you about this stretch of coast said two things: “they’re strong enough to drown a horse” and “if one ...

name: Karakoncolos
flags: elemental_affinity:None — the karakoncolos exists in the gap between elemental signatures, in the dead zones where Kin-sense cannot reach
environment: Kin-Sense Dead Zones (areas where elemental resonance has been drained, suppressed, or corrupted — typically near Dómnathar surface-breach points, in the ruins of Dómnathar outposts, and at the edges of False Silence fields)
description: The dead zone is wrong. Not dead — dead zones are common in Oreka, places where Kin-sense returns nothing, where the elemental resonance has been drained or suppressed or was never there. Those are empty. This place is not empty. This place is full, and you cannot hear what is filling it. Something stands in the silence where your Kin-sense should be, and it is looking at you through the gap in the world.

name: Kinnari
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Youngest Brother (Air) — the kinnari is the waterfall-song, the place where water and air create music
environment: Great River and Sea Falls (the major waterfalls of Oreka — the Sea Falls along the Pekakarlik coast, the Grand Falls on the Great River, and the cascading river-systems of the Giant’s Teeth; always at waterfalls, never in still water or open sky)
description: The music comes from the waterfall’s mist. Not from behind the waterfall, not from above it — from the mist itself, from the place where falling water becomes air. The voice is singing, and the voice is the most beautiful thing you have ever heard. It is a woman’s voice, high and clear and resonant in a way that no mortal throat produces, and when the mist parts, you see her: bird-legged, bird-winged, woman-bodied, hovering in the spray-column with effortless grace, her feathers iridescent wi...

name: Kitsune
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — mutable; the kitsune’s air-resonance shifts and changes like the wind, never quite the same twice, reflecting the fey’s shapeshifting nature
environment: Great Forests (the deciduous and mixed forests of Hasura territory — forest-edges, shrine-clearings, ancient game-trails, and the borderlands where managed forest meets wild wood)
organization: Solitary or skulk (2–4, always related — a parent and offspring, or siblings)
advancement: 7–12 HD (Medium — each additional HD represents a new tail, to a maximum of nine)
description: The fox was there a moment ago. You saw it — a red fox, large, sitting at the tree-line with its tail curled around its feet, watching you with an intelligence that foxes should not have. You blinked. Now it is a woman — or was a woman, or is becoming a woman, or is something in between that your brain is trying to resolve into one shape or the other and failing. She smiles at you with the fox’s eyes in a woman’s face, and you understand that the shape was never the point.

name: Klatterkin
flags: elemental_affinity:None — construct; breach resonance indicates extra-dimensional origin of animating principle
environment: Any (wherever goblins are — klatterkin attach themselves to goblin communities and travel with them; found in goblin settlements, camps, and warbands across every region of Oreka)
description: The goblin warband’s camp is unusually well-equipped.
