That is the first thing the scout notices — not scavenged armour and stolen weapons, but purpose-built equipment: a ballista assembled from scavenged parts that somehow works better than it should, a camp-perimeter alarm made of bones and wire that triggers with mechanical precision, and in the centre of the camp, tended by two goblins with the careful hands of acolytes, a contraption. It is three feet tall, made of wood and metal and teet...

name: Kobold
flags: elemental_affinity:None — kobolds exist outside the elemental framework, but their reptilian physiology and deep-underground habitation give them a faint, false earth-resonance that reads as “part of the stone”
environment: Underground and Dómnathar Tunnels (natural cave systems, mine-tunnels, and — significantly — the outer corridors of Dómnathar tunnel-networks, where kobold warrens serve as a buffer-zone between the Deceivers and the surface)
organization: Gang (4–9), band (10–100 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1 leader of 4th–6th level), warband (10–24 plus 2–4 dire weasels), or tribe (40–400 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1–2 lieutenants of 4th–5th level plus 1 leader of 6th–8th level plus 10–24 dire weasels)
description: The tunnel is too small for you. That is the point — everything in kobold territory is too small for you, too narrow, too low-ceilinged, too tight to swing a weapon. You are on your hands and knees in a passage two feet wide and eighteen inches tall, and somewhere ahead of you, in the dark, something is waiting with a crossbow loaded and a tripwire set and the patient, ancient hatred of a species that has survived everything by being smaller, faster, and more vindictive than anything that hun...

name: Kodama
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — the deep, slow earth-resonance of a living tree’s connection to the soil
environment: Great Forests (the interior of the oldest forests — wherever a tree has stood long enough and grown deep enough to develop a kodama; never in young growth, plantations, or recently-cleared areas)
organization: Solitary (one per tree, always) or grove (3–20, one per tree in a kodama-grove)
advancement: 1–3 HD (Tiny — larger kodama inhabit larger, older trees)
description: The sound is not a sound. It is a vibration — a resonance that comes from the tree itself, from the ring of wood inside the bark, from the sap moving through channels that have been open for five hundred years. If you press your ear to the trunk and listen with more than your ears, you hear it: a pulse, slow and patient, like a heartbeat that measures its time in seasons rather than seconds. The kodama is not in the tree. The kodama is the tree’s awareness of itself.

name: Kraken
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — abyssal; the kraken’s water-resonance is deep-ocean, immense, and ancient — the Lady’s domain at its most vast and indifferent
environment: Deep Apelian Sea (the Kailian Trench and the abyssal depths of the open Apelian — water so deep that sunlight has never reached it, where the pressure alone kills most creatures and the kraken is the apex intelligence)
description: The Apelian Sea has things in it that the surface-folk do not discuss. The Na’wasua know. The abyssal fishermen know — the ones who cast their lines into water so deep that the line runs out before the weight hits bottom. The thing at the bottom of the Kailian Trench is not a fish. It is not a whale. It is a mind attached to arms, and the arms are each two hundred feet long, and the mind has been thinking for longer than the Na’wasua have had a word for “old.”

name: Krasue
flags: elemental_affinity:None — undead
environment: Deepwater Marches (the wetland interior of the Deepwater Marches — stagnant pools, reed-beds, and the foggy marsh-paths that halfling fishing-communities use to navigate between settlements)
description: The light in the marsh is not a lantern. It moves wrong — bobbing, drifting, hovering at head-height with the lazy motion of something that is not subject to wind. When it moves closer, you see what is carrying the light: a woman’s head. Just the head — floating, trailing a mass of viscera beneath it like a jellyfish trails its tentacles. The organs glow faintly from within, producing the light that lured you here. The face is beautiful. The face is always beautiful. The face is smiling.

name: Lamassu
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords — primordial; the unbroken harmony of the Giant age, carried forward in living stone and wind
environment: Giant Ruins (the great ruin-complexes of the Infinite Desert — the Sunken Thrones, Tharok’s Pillar, and the partially-collapsed temple-cities of the Glass Wastes’ edge; rare sightings at Giant-era bridges and gate-complexes in the Giant’s Teeth foothills)
description: The ruin is silent — that particular silence of places where someone carved stone with purpose ten thousand years ago and the stone remembers. You round the fallen pillar and something is watching you from the colonnade’s shadow. A body like a bull cast in granite, shoulders broad enough to block the passage, wings folded tight against its flanks — eagle’s wings, massive, the feathers carved from living bronze. And the face. The face is not a bull’s face. It is older than anything you have me...

name: Lamia
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of Fire (Fire) — corrupted; desert heat twisted inward, consuming rather than warming
environment: Infinite Desert (the deep dune-seas and abandoned oases between Pekakarlik waystations — particularly the Glass Wastes, where the ruins offer hiding-places and the oases draw prey; rare sightings along the Scorchspire foothills’ desert edge)
description: The woman standing at the oasis edge is beautiful. You notice that first — the dark hair, the amber eyes, the confident stillness with which she watches your approach. You notice second that she is alone in the deep desert, a day’s ride from the nearest Pekakarlik waystation, with no supplies, no mount, and no footprints leading to where she stands. You notice third — and this is the last useful thing you will notice, because by now the Wisdom is already draining from behind your eyes — that ...

name: Lasa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — gentle; river-current and willow-shade, the slow music of water over stone
environment: Great River (the quiet bends, willow-pools, and sacred confluences of the Great River system — particularly the Eruskan stretches between Twin Rivers and the Sea Falls, where the river widens and the current slows)
organization: Solitary, pair, or confluence (3–8 at a major river junction)
description: The river is quieter here — the current softens where the willow-roots meet the water, and the evening light turns the surface to hammered copper. You see her sitting on the stones at the water’s edge, bare feet in the current, combing her hair with a comb made of river-shell. She is not quite solid. The light passes through her edges. When she turns to look at you, her eyes are the colour of deep water, and the song she has been humming settles into your chest like a second heartbeat. The Gr...

name: Leshy
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) — ancient; root-deep and canopy-high, the living forest from soil to sky
environment: Great Forests (the deepest interior of the Hasura canopy and Pasua ground-level forests — ancient-growth stands where the trees are old enough to have names; rare sightings in Kovaka coastal forests and the forest-steppe border)
organization: Solitary or moot (2–4, extremely rare — leshiye gather only to resolve territorial disputes)
description: The forest is watching you. Not in the way that forests always watch — the incurious attention of birds and insects and the slow awareness of old trees. This is focused. This is personal. You have been walking for three hours and you are back where you started, though you have not turned once. The blaze-marks you cut into the bark an hour ago are gone. The path you were following does not exist. Somewhere ahead, in the green shadow between the oaks, something enormous shifts its weight and th...

name: Leviathan-Shard
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — alien; so ancient and so deep that it reads as something from before the Lady shaped the waters
environment: Any coastal or wetland (Leviathan-shards wash ashore on the Apelian Sea coast and are found in free goblin camps throughout the Deepwater Marches; specimens have been transported as far as the Tidebloom Reach by goblin traders)
organization: Solitary or clutch (2–4, usually in a goblin camp)
description: You smell it before you see it — brine and depth and something older, a pressure-smell like the deep ocean forced into a space too small to contain it. The goblin camp ahead has arranged its tents in a wide semicircle around a pit lined with wet stones, and in the pit there is a thing. A mass of translucent flesh, pale blue and shot through with veins of bioluminescence that pulse in a slow rhythm unrelated to anything on land. It is the size of a cart-horse, and it is alive in a way that mak...

name: Lindworm
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — deep; the slow pressure of mountains, the heat of the world’s bones beneath the crust
environment: Giant’s Teeth Deep Shafts (the deepest mine-shafts, natural fissures, and geothermal vents below Vestri mining depth — particularly the Undershafts beneath Stoneheart, the flooded deep-tunnels of Ironwell, and the volcanic fissures of the inner Giant’s Teeth)
description: The shaft goes deeper than the Vestri mapped it. The stonework ended three levels up — down here, the walls are raw mountain, the seams of ore glinting in your lantern-light like veins in a living body. The air is warm and wet and smells of acid. You hear it before you see it: a vast, slow scraping, like a ship’s hull dragging across rock, and the sound of something breathing through a mouth full of venom. The lindworm fills the tunnel. Its body is serpentine and armoured in scales the colour...

name: Locathah
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — steady; deep-current and thermal warmth, the patient rhythm of tidal cycles
environment: Deep Apelian Trenches (the geothermal vent communities along the Apelian Sea’s deepest fissures — particularly the submerged Giant-era aqueducts and flooded temple-complexes that provide structure for locathah settlements; smaller communities in the Kailian Sea’s northern deep-water shelves)
organization: Company (2–4), patrol (11–20 plus 2 3rd-level sergeants and 1 leader of 3rd–6th level), or tribe (30–100 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 3rd-level sergeant per 10 adults plus 5 5th-level lieutenants plus 3 7th-level captains)
description: The water is warm here — the deep Apelian currents rising through thermal vents in the trench floor, carrying minerals and heat into the crushing dark. Your water breathing spell holds, but the pressure aches behind your eyes. Movement below: a dozen figures rising from the coral-crusted ledge of a Giant-era aqueduct, their bodies sleek and scaled, their eyes reflecting your magelight with a flat, inhuman calm. They carry spears of sharpened coral tipped with Giant-ruin bronze, and they move ...

name: Maero (Brute)
flags: elemental_affinity:None — artificial earth-attunement, cosmetic only
environment: Mountain Valleys (the densely-forested mountain valleys of Kovaka coastal territory — the steep-walled river-valleys, old-growth ravines, and fog-bound coastal forests of the southwestern Great Forests)
organization: Solitary, pair, or band (3–5, rare — degraded War-era formation)
description: The Kovaka call them te tangata mohoao — the wild people — but there is nothing of a person in what steps out of the tree-line. It is eight feet of matted hair and raw-boned muscle, its arms too long for its body, its hands thick-fingered and blunt as mallets. The face is wrong in the particular way that all Brute faces are wrong: close enough to Kin to trigger recognition, far enough from Kin to trigger revulsion. It smells of rotting bark and swamp-water and something chemical beneath both ...

name: Magma Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — volcanic; liquid stone made malicious, the Scorchspires’ smallest and most irritating ambassadors
environment: Scorchspire Caldera (the active volcanic vents, lava tubes, and caldera rims of the Scorchspires — particularly the Rarozhki-adjacent vents where elemental activity is highest)
description: The caldera vent cracks open and something small climbs out, dripping with molten rock that cools on its body in streaks of black and red. It is two feet tall, vaguely humanoid, and made of cooling magma — each movement cracking its skin to reveal the orange glow beneath. It grins at you with a mouth full of obsidian teeth and speaks in a voice like a campfire settling. Behind it, three more are climbing out.

name: Magmin
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) — pure; the elemental flame given form, innocent and incendiary
environment: Scorchspires (active lava-tubes, volcanic fissures, and the deep caldera floors where molten rock is accessible — always near active magma sources)
description: The lava-tube glows from within — not the dull red of cooling rock, but the bright, shifting orange of something moving. A shape detaches itself from the wall: small, humanoid, and burning. Not on fire — made of fire, given form by a thin shell of cooling rock that cracks and reforms with every step. Where it walks, the stone beneath its feet softens and glows. It looks at you with eyes that are holes into something incandescent, and you understand instantly that this creature does not know i...

name: Mamlambo
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — corrupted; stagnant, hungry, the patience of water that has forgotten how to flow
environment: River-Steppe Border (the wide, slow-moving river crossings where the Great River’s tributaries meet the Eternal Steppe — the fording-points, gravel shallows, and seasonal flood-pools used by steppe herds and Mytroan caravans)
description: The river crossing has been safe for a decade. The Warg packs ford here every season and the Mytroan riders use it as a watering-point on the steppe-circuit. Then the horses stopped drinking. The Wargs crossed upstream, three miles out of their way, and when asked why, the pack-leader said only: “The river has teeth now.” You crouch at the bank and the water looks normal — brown, shallow, running smooth over gravel. You cannot see what is lying on the bottom, because the thing on the bottom h...

name: Manananggal
flags: elemental_affinity:None — undead, Silence Breach-origin
environment: Silence Breach Zones (the Kin-sense dead zones near Dómnathar surface-breach points — particularly in the Gatefall Reach and the outer Kinsweave, where the residual metaphysical damage of the Deceivers’ War entry-point creates conditions that sustain this specific form of undead)
description: You find the lower half first. It is standing upright in a Dómnathar-ruined basement, legs together, trunk severed cleanly at the waist, and it is warm. Blood pools slowly at the separation point but does not spill — as though the body is waiting for something to return. The cellar smells of vinegar and old blood and the particular sterile absence of a place where Kin-sense dies. You hear wings above you — heavy, wet, membranous wings — and something laughs in the dark with a voice that used ...

name: Manticore
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of Fire (Fire) — predatory; the dry heat of sun-baked stone, the patience of rock that waits to fall
environment: Warm Hills and Marshes (the low, scrub-covered hills between the Eternal Steppe and the Scorchspires — the transitional terrain where grassland gives way to volcanic foothills; secondary populations along the Tidebloom Reach’s drier margins)
description: The thing on the ridge has the body of a lion, the face of a man, and a tail bristling with spines the length of crossbow bolts. It has been watching your party for the better part of an hour with the patient, calculating intelligence of a predator that understands geometry. It is not attacking because the range is wrong and it knows the range is wrong. When you cross the open ground between the tree-line and the ridge — that killing-ground of bare earth and low scrub where there is nothing t...

name: Mapinguari
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — corrupted; ancient, sick, the resonance of soil that has turned toxic
environment: Deepwater Marches (the dense interior jungle of the Marches — the oldest-growth, most inaccessible regions where goblin settlements are sparse and the canopy blocks all sunlight at ground level)
description: The jungle went quiet three days ago. Not the quiet of nightfall or the quiet of a predator passing through — the quiet of everything leaving. The birds are gone. The monkeys are gone. The insects are muted. Something enormous is moving through the Deepwater canopy, and the stench reached you before the sound did: a rotting, animal musk so powerful it burns the back of your throat. You see it through the trees — bipedal, shaggy, taller than a man on horseback, with one milky eye set in the ce...

name: Mazzikin
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — mischievous; the air that steals your hat, the wind that snuffs your candle
environment: Any (mazzikin are found wherever goblins gather — goblin camps, trading-posts, and caravans in the Deepwater Marches, but also goblin-Kin border-communities, Eruskan market-towns with goblin populations, and isolated settlements where the boundary between fey and mortal is thin)
description: You cannot see them. That is the point — mazzikin are invisible, bodiless, and everywhere, and the only evidence of their presence is the mischief. Your bootlaces are tied together. Your waterskin is empty though you filled it an hour ago. The goblin trader’s pack has been rearranged so that the heavy items are on top and the fragile items are on the bottom. From somewhere near the ceiling, something giggles — a high, musical laugh that sounds like a child who has never been told no.

name: Medusa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — corrupted; petrification as a perverted expression of earth-affinity, turning living flesh to stone against its will
environment: Ruins and Marshes (abandoned structures throughout Oreka — Giant ruins in the Infinite Desert, ruined border-settlements in the Kinsweave, crumbling watchtowers in the Tidebloom Reach; always in built environments where stone is plentiful and prey can be lured into enclosed spaces)
description: The gallery is a sculpture-garden — or you thought it was. The figures are too detailed, too varied, too individual to be carved. The dwarf in the corner has a crack in his breastplate that no sculptor would include. The elf at the entrance has an expression of dawning recognition on her face that no art could capture. The woman at the centre of the gallery is veiled. She asks if you would like to see her collection. She asks very politely.

name: Merfolk
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — tidal; the steady pull of coastal currents, salt and warmth and sunlight through shallow water
environment: Apelian Sea (the shallow coastal waters, tidal flats, coral reefs, and estuaries of the Apelian Sea — particularly the warmer southern reaches where coral growth provides shelter and the shallows are productive)
organization: Company (2–4), patrol (11–20 plus 2 3rd-level sergeants and 1 leader of 3rd–6th level), or community (20–80 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 3rd-level sergeant per 10 adults plus 1 leader of 6th–8th level)
description: The water is warm and shallow here — the Apelian tidal flats, where the sea meets the coral shelf and the light reaches the bottom. A face breaks the surface ten yards out, watching you with dark, calm eyes. Then another. Then six more. They are not threatening — they are simply there, the way the sea is there, with the absolute confidence of creatures in their element. Their upper bodies are Kin-like — close enough to human that you could mistake them at a distance — and their lower bodies a...

name: Mimic
flags: elemental_affinity:None — mimics exist outside the elemental framework entirely; their physiology registers as an absence that is not Dómnathar void but simply nothing
environment: Giant Ruins (the interior rooms, corridors, and vaults of Giant-era ruin-complexes throughout the Infinite Desert — any structure where there are objects to mimic and prey to attract; secondary populations in Vestri deep-mines and Dómnathar-adjacent tunnel-systems)
description: The chest is the only thing in the room that is not covered in dust. You notice this, but you notice it too late — your hand is already on the lid, and the lid is already on your hand. The surface beneath your fingers is warm and wet, and the chest is opening in a direction that chests do not open, and the inside of the chest is full of teeth.

name: Minotaur
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — deep; the resonance of underground stone, of passages carved by creatures that live where light does not reach
environment: Underground Labyrinths (the interconnected tunnel-systems beneath the Giant’s Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Kinsweave — natural cave networks expanded and modified by minotaur habitation into deliberate mazes; some populations inhabit Giant-era sub-surface complexes that were designed as labyrinths)
description: The labyrinth was not built for you. It was built for something larger, something that moves through these corridors with the confidence of a creature that has memorised every turn. You have been lost for two hours. You hear it breathe — a deep, slow rasp of something big drawing air through a bovine muzzle — and then the sound of hooves on stone, accelerating.

name: Mngwa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — shadow; earth-resonance filtered through something dark, the weight of stone in the space where light fails
environment: Scorchspire Lowlands (the transitional terrain between the Scorchspires’ volcanic slopes and the settled lowlands of Taraf-Imro territory — the scrubland, ash-meadows, and low thorn-forests where volcanic soil meets steppe grass)
description: The lowlands are quiet tonight. The Taraf-Imro herders brought the cattle in early — they could feel it coming, the way experienced herders feel a predator’s approach not in any one sense but in all of them at once: the cattle’s nervousness, the birds going silent, the quality of the firelight changing as though something were standing just beyond its reach, watching. The mngwa does not hunt from ambush. It hunts from impossibility — it is there and then it is not, it is shadow and then it is...

name: Mohrg
flags: elemental_affinity:None — undead
environment: Kinsweave Ruins (the abandoned border-settlements, burned villages, and War-era fortifications of the Kinsweave — the region where the northern Deceiver host’s passage left the densest concentration of unburied dead and corrupted ground)
description: The ruin smelled wrong before you entered it — a sickly-sweet rot that no amount of wind would carry away, layered over the cold-stone scent of the Kinsweave’s abandoned border-settlements. Inside, the bodies are arranged. Not piled, not scattered — arranged, in a manner that suggests intelligence, purpose, and a deeply unpleasant sense of humour. In the centre of the arrangement, the thing that made it stands upright: a skeleton wrapped in its own entrails, its ribcage open like a birdcage, ...

name: Monstrous Spider
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — faint; the quiet attunement of small creatures that live in dark places close to stone
environment: Any (monstrous spiders are found throughout Oreka — Great Forests, underground cave-systems, Giant ruins, Vestri mines, Dómnathar tunnel-fringes, and any enclosed environment where webs can be anchored; specific subspecies favour specific environments)
description: The web is not visible until you are in it. The silk is the colour of shadow and it clings to everything — armour, hair, skin — with an adhesive strength that is entirely out of proportion to its apparent fragility. Something moves at the edge of your torchlight. It has too many legs and too many eyes and it is larger than you want it to be.

name: Mud Menders
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — constructive; the intersection of water and earth as building material, the quiet industry of riverbanks repairing themselves
environment: Marshes and River Deltas (the Deepwater Marches’ mudflats, the Great River’s flood-plains, and the tidal estuaries where fresh water meets the Apelian — anywhere that river-mud accumulates in sufficient depth and elemental water-earth energy is concentrated)
description: The riverbank is repairing itself. Where the spring flood tore a section away, leaving raw earth and exposed roots, something is working — dozens of small, wet, vaguely humanoid shapes made of river-mud and clay, moving with the busy purposefulness of ants. They are packing mud into the wound, weaving roots through the fill, and smoothing the surface with hands that are more suggestion than anatomy. One of them notices you watching. It stops, tilts its featureless head, and extends a hand tha...

name: Mummy
flags: elemental_affinity:None — undead; but the body was once Giant, and traces of the all-four-element resonance linger in the preservation-runes like an echo in a sealed room
environment: Giant Tombs (the sealed burial-complexes and mausoleum-vaults of Giant-era cities throughout the Infinite Desert — the Sunken Thrones, the Dust-Vaults beneath Tharok’s Pillar, and the deep tomb-complexes of the Giant’s Teeth foothills)
description: The sarcophagus is not dwarven. It is Giant — carved from a single block of basalt in proportions that no Kin hands shaped, covered in rune-script that predates written Dwarven by millennia. The lid has been moved. From inside, the smell of preservation-resin and dust and something else — something dry and electric that makes the hair on your arms stand up. The thing that climbs out does not rush. It has been waiting for longer than your civilisation has existed, and it has the patience of st...

name: Mushussu
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords — fading; the all-element resonance of the Giant age, attenuated by millennia of isolation, guttering like a candle in its last hours
environment: Desert Ruins (the gate-complexes, processional avenues, and entrance-ways of the most intact Giant-era ruin-cities in the Infinite Desert — the Sunken Thrones, Tharok’s Pillar, and the partially-buried temple-complexes of the Glass Wastes)
organization: Solitary, pair, or gate-vigil (3–5 at a major gate-complex)
description: The ruin-gate is guarded. The guardian is not Giant — it is something the Giants made, or something that lived with the Giants and stayed after they were gone. It has the body of a scaled serpent, the forelegs of a lion, the hind-legs of an eagle, and a neck that arches upward like a crane’s, capped with a horned head whose eyes glow with a faint, steady amber light. It has been sitting in front of this gate for ten thousand years. The bones at its feet suggest that it takes its duties seriou...

name: Näck
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — dark; the pull of deep water, the current beneath the surface that drags you under while the surface stays calm
environment: Sea Falls (the great coastal waterfalls along the Pekakarlik sea-fall trade-route — the plunge-pools, mist-caves, and spray-hollows where freshwater meets the Apelian; secondary presence at isolated inland waterfalls in the Great River system, always at falls with deep plunge-pools)
description: The Sea Falls roar, and beneath the roar there is music. Not the kinnari’s waterfall-song — you have heard that, and it was beautiful in a way that healed. This is beautiful in a way that takes. A violin, played from behind the curtain of falling water by hands you cannot see, producing a melody so perfect it hollows you out. Your feet are wet. You have walked into the pool at the falls’ base without deciding to. The water is at your knees. The music is louder. The player steps through the sp...

name: Nāga (Oreka)
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — ancient; the deep riverbed, the slow power of water that has carved stone for millennia
environment: Deep Great River (the deepest, oldest stretches of the Great River — the canyon-passages where the river runs through stone gorges hundreds of feet deep, the confluence-pools where major tributaries meet, and the sacred deep-holes that Pekakarlik rivermen mark on their charts with respect-symbols)
description: The river is deep here — so deep the bottom vanishes into green-black shadow, and the current runs slow and heavy as though the water itself were old. The Pekakarlik rivermen cut their engines and let the barge drift. They have been quiet for the last mile, speaking only in hand-signs, and when you ask why, the barge-master points to the water and puts a finger to his lips. Something vast is coiled on the riverbed below you — a serpentine shape forty feet long, its scales iridescent even in t...

name: Nak
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — brackish; the murky, salt-tinged water where river meets sea, neither fully fresh nor fully salt
environment: Pekakarlik Docks (the timber-pylon dock-complexes of Pekakarlik coastal and estuarine trading-posts — the pilings, jetties, and harbour structures where river-barges meet sea-going vessels along the Apelian coast)
description: The dock is old Pekakarlik construction — heavy timber pylons driven into the estuary-mud, planked with salt-treated oak, worn smooth by a century of barge-keels and bare feet. At the dock’s end, where the pylons meet the water, something lives between the pilings. The children see it first — they always do — a crocodilian shape the size of a large dog, long-snouted and dark-scaled, watching the dock-traffic from the shadows between the timbers with bright, unblinking eyes. It is not a crocod...

name: Nang Tani
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) — nurturing; the dark soil of cultivated groves, the patient water that feeds roots, growth expressed as care
environment: Halfling Orchards (the fruit-groves and crop-orchards of the Deepwater Marches and Tidebloom Reach halfling communities — particularly old-growth banana, mango, and breadfruit groves that have been tended for generations)
description: The banana grove is older than the halfling settlement that tends it. The trees are tall and thick-trunked, their broad leaves casting the kind of shade that turns midday into twilight, and the air beneath them is sweet with the smell of ripening fruit and damp earth. You notice one tree at the grove’s heart that is different — not larger, but present in a way the others are not. A green silk ribbon is tied around its trunk, and at its base, a scattering of fresh flowers, a small clay cup of ...

name: Nasnas
flags: elemental_affinity:None — aberrant; a creature split from the elemental framework, registering on Kin-sense as half a signature, a broken signal that never resolves
environment: Na’wasua Groves (the tidal-forest groves and mangrove-islands of Na’wasua sea-elf territory along the Apelian coast — particularly the remote outer groves where the forest meets the sea and the boundary between land and water is ambiguous)
description: The Na’wasua grove is wrong. The sea-elves noticed it first — the resonance shifted, the water in the tidal-pool tastes sour, and something is moving at the edge of the tree-line with a gait that is not animal, not Kin, and not right. It hops. That is the first thing you notice — it hops, because it has one leg. One arm. Half a face, split vertically, so the left side is smooth flesh and the right side is exposed muscle and bone. It is fast — faster than anything with one leg should be — and ...

name: Níðhöggr-Kin
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — inverted; the anti-resonance of something that consumes earth rather than expressing it, entropy given draconic form
environment: Giant’s Teeth Roots (the deepest subterranean levels beneath the Giant’s Teeth — below Vestri mining depth, below lindworm territory, in the root-halls where petrified ancient forest meets living stone)
description: The root-hall goes deeper than the dwarves mapped. Below the Vestri deep-shafts, below the lindworm tunnels, below everything that lives in the dark by choice, there is a place where the mountain’s roots meet the world’s roots — where stone becomes something older, something organic, something that grew rather than formed. The rock here is threaded with veins of petrified wood the size of aqueducts, remnants of trees that were old when the Giants were young. And something is eating them. You ...

name: Nixie
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — gentle; the clear water of shallow lakes, sunlight on the surface, the warmth of still pools
environment: Rivers and Lakes (freshwater lakes, slow rivers, mill-ponds, and spring-fed pools throughout the Great River system and the Deepwater Marches — anywhere that still, clear freshwater collects in sufficient depth)
description: The lake is clear and shallow at the edges, the kind of place where children wade and fishermen cast from the bank. You see the light first — a flicker of green-gold beneath the surface, like sunlight catching a fish-scale, except the light moves with purpose. A head breaks the surface: small, angular, green-skinned, with large dark eyes and hair the colour of waterweed. She treads water effortlessly and regards you with the frank curiosity of something that owns this lake and considers you a...

name: Noppera-Bō
flags: elemental_affinity:None — aberrant; a creature that registers on Kin-sense as a blank space in the shape of a person, like a silhouette cut from the world
environment: Great Forests (Tanuki groves and the surrounding old-growth forest — particularly the twilight-zones between Tanuki settlements and the deeper forest, where the lantern-light ends and the shadows begin)
description: The path through the Tanuki grove is well-lit — paper lanterns strung between the trees, their warm light turning the forest-floor into a patchwork of gold and shadow. You have been walking for twenty minutes and you are beginning to suspect that the path is longer than it should be. The figure ahead of you on the path is a Tanuki woman — round-faced, small, carrying a basket of mushrooms. She has been walking ahead of you since you entered the grove. She has not turned around. You call out. ...

name: Nuckelavee
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — inverted; the absolute negation of water’s nurturing principle, corruption given physical form
environment: Tidebloom Reach (the salt-marshes, tidal flats, and coastal wetlands of the Tidebloom Reach — the border-zone where brackish water meets the Apelian Sea, particularly the areas where the tides carry pollution and decay inland)
description: The Tidebloom stinks of rot. Not the clean rot of marshland decomposition — something chemical, something that burns the sinuses and leaves a taste like copper and bile on the back of your tongue. The water has turned the colour of old blood. The fish are floating belly-up in the shallows, and the reed-beds are black and dying in concentric rings radiating from a point you cannot yet see. Then you see it — rising from the fouled water like something being born from a wound. Horse and rider, f...

name: Nymph
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords — pristine; the unbroken elemental harmony of the world as it was meant to be, expressed through living beauty
environment: Pristine Groves (isolated pockets of elemental perfection scattered across Oreka — old-growth hollows, mountain springs, forest-clearings, and lakeside meadows where the elemental balance has never been disrupted, usually far from Kin settlement and often concealed by natural terrain)
description: The grove is a place that should not exist. You are in the foothills of the Giant’s Teeth, where the forest is thin and the soil is rocky and nothing grows taller than scrub-oak — but here, in this hollow between two ridges, there is a grove of ancient trees surrounding a pool of water so clear it looks like a window into the earth. The air is different — warmer, sweeter, thick with the scent of flowers that have no business blooming at this altitude. Everything here is more alive than it sho...

name: Ochre Jelly
flags: elemental_affinity:None — oozes exist entirely outside the elemental framework; they register on Kin-sense as a gap, a place where resonance should be but isn’t
environment: Underground (Giant-built tunnel-complexes, Vestri deep-mines, natural cave-systems, Dómnathar tunnel-fringes — any enclosed underground space with organic matter to consume)
description: The tunnel floor is wet. Not with water — with something that has the colour of old amber and the consistency of warm tar, and it is moving. You notice it because the rat you were following has stopped running. The rat is dissolving. The amber substance has risen over the rat’s legs, is climbing its body, and the rat is thrashing but cannot pull free. By the time the rat stops thrashing, the amber has consumed everything except the bones, which it leaves behind, clean and polished, on the tun...

name: Ogre
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — dim; a faint, dull earth-resonance that reads as “rock that doesn’t know it’s alive”
environment: Temperate Hills (the foothills and hill-country surrounding the Giant’s Teeth, Scorchspire margins, and the hilly transition-zones between forest and steppe — anywhere with caves for shelter and livestock within raiding distance)
description: The campfire was not built by someone who knows how to build a campfire. The logs are too big, stacked wrong, and the fire is more bonfire than cooking-fire — throwing light and smoke a hundred yards in every direction. The bones around the fire-pit are mixed: deer, sheep, dog, and something disturbingly humanoid. The smell is appalling. Then the ground trembles, and the thing that built the fire comes back through the tree-line carrying a cow under one arm. It is nine feet tall, pot-bellied,...

name: Ogre Mage
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) — cold; the sharp elemental signature of mountain-cold, ice on stone, the intersection where earth and water become something harder than either
environment: Cold Mountain Lairs (high passes, glacial caves, and mountain-top ruins in the upper Giant’s Teeth and northern Scorchspires — above the tree-line, in terrain too cold and remote for most settlements)
description: The cold comes first — a drop in temperature sharp enough to see your breath, in a mountain pass where the air was merely cool a moment ago. Then the voice, speaking flawless Eruskan from somewhere above you, offers passage through the pass in exchange for a “small consideration.” The speaker steps from behind a rock-face that should not have concealed anything that large: nine feet tall, blue-skinned, white-haired, wearing lacquered armour of a style you do not recognise, carrying a glaive a...

name: Olgoi-Khorkhoi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — deep, sick; a churning, acidic earth-resonance that reads as “the ground digesting itself”
environment: Infinite Desert (the deep hardpan flats between dune-fields — sun-baked clay-pan stretches where nothing grows, where the earth is cracked and dry, and where the olgoi-khorkhoi burrows beneath the surface waiting for vibrations)
description: The Mytroan scout dismounts fifty paces from the depression in the hardpan. He kneels and presses his palm flat against the baked earth — a gesture you recognise from steppe-rider tradition, the earth-reading that Mytroan scouts use to detect burrowing threats. His face goes white. He stands, backs toward his horse without turning around, mounts, and says one word: khorkhoi. The other riders wheel their horses immediately. No one argues. No one asks questions. Everyone rides.

name: Ōmukade
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — toxic; heavy, metallic earth-resonance with a chemical-burn quality, like copper ore exposed to acid
environment: Deep Forest (Tanuki groves and the surrounding old-growth interior of the Great Forests — the densest, darkest forest-floor zones where fallen trees create tunnel-like passages and the canopy blocks all sunlight)
description: The centipede fills the tunnel. That is the first and most important fact — it fills the tunnel from wall to wall, from floor to ceiling, and it is moving toward you with a sound like a thousand knives being drawn across stone simultaneously. It is the colour of dried blood, its body is as thick as a wine-barrel, and every segment carries a pair of legs that end in hooks sharp enough to gouge stone. Its head is a nightmare of mandibles and compound eyes, and when it opens its jaws, the air fi...

name: Oni
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — dark, primal; deep-forest earth, the resonance of soil that has been dark since the world began, where the forest-floor is older than the trees growing on it
environment: Deep Forests (Hasura territory and beyond — the oldest, densest interior of the Great Forests where the canopy blocks all direct sunlight and the undergrowth is primordial)
description: The deep forest changes character past the Hasura border-markers. The trees are older, the canopy is lower, and the undergrowth is thick enough to hide a horse. The Hasura rangers who guided you this far have stopped. They will not go further. They point to a mark on a tree — a claw-mark at nine feet, old and deep — and speak the name quietly, as though the creature might hear: oni. In the forest beyond the marker, something laughs. It is a deep laugh, rich with amusement, and it comes from t...

name: Ooze Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — stagnant; the unmoving, brackish water of marshes where nothing flows, things decay, and the water itself seems to have given up
environment: Apelian Marshes (the coastal wetlands and tidal marshes where the Apelian Sea meets the Deepwater Marches — brackish pools, reed-beds, mangrove-shallows, and anywhere that stagnant water accumulates in sufficient volume)
description: The marsh is the wrong colour. Not the usual brown-green of Apelian wetland — a sickly, iridescent sheen like oil on water, and it moves, shifting across the surface in patterns that are too regular to be current and too slow to be wind. A bubble rises from the muck and pops with a sound like a wet cough. Something climbs out of the bubble — small, dripping, translucent, with a body that seems unable to decide whether it is liquid or solid. It has eyes, of a sort — dark spots in the gelatinou...

name: Orc
flags: elemental_affinity:None — wild static; orcs, like goblins, register on Kin-sense as static rather than a clean elemental signature, though orc static is coarser and more aggressive than goblin static
environment: Any (hill-country, cave-systems, ruins, forest-edges, and the margins between Kin territories — anywhere that provides shelter, defensible ground, and access to raiding targets)
organization: Gang (2–4), squad (11–20 plus 2 3rd-level sergeants and 1 leader of 4th–6th level), or band (30–100 plus 150% noncombatants plus 1 3rd-level sergeant per 10 adults, 5 5th-level lieutenants, and 3 7th-level captains)
description: The raiders come out of the ravine at a run — fast, organised, and angry. There are a dozen of them, grey-skinned and heavy-jawed, wearing patchwork armour assembled from three different cultures’ discarded kit. The one in front carries an axe that was Orean-forged before someone reground the edge to a crueler angle. They do not shout. They do not threaten. They come in silent, trained, and focused, which means these are not the disorganised raiders the border-patrols warned you about. These ...

name: Otyugh
flags: elemental_affinity:None — aberrant; otyughs register on Kin-sense as a faint, unpleasant signal that doesn’t map to any elemental framework
environment: City Sewers and Dungeons (the sewage-systems, refuse-pits, and underground waste-channels of Eruskan cities, Vestri mining-camps, and any settlement large enough to generate concentrated organic waste)
description: The sewer smells exactly as bad as you expected, but the thing in the sewer smells worse. It occupies the junction where three tunnels meet — a bulbous, three-legged mass of mottled brown-grey flesh, waist-deep in the effluent, with two long tentacles rising from its body like the antennae of something that evolved specifically to live in places no other creature would tolerate. Its third appendage — a thick, eyestalk on a muscular neck — swivels toward you with the calm, unsurprised attentio...

name: Owlbear
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) — fused; a dual resonance that reads as two signals imperfectly merged, as though something forced bear-earth and owl-air together and the result never fully integrated
environment: Great Forests (the mid-depth Great Forests — deeper than the forest-edge where Pasua patrol, shallower than the deep interior where oni and dire tigers dominate — the broad middle-zone of mixed deciduous and coniferous forest that is the Great Forests’ most common terrain)
description: The thing about the owlbear is that you hear both halves before you see either. The hoot comes first — deep, resonant, wrong, because it comes from a throat the size of a barrel rather than a bird’s syrinx. Then the growl beneath the hoot, the ursine rumble that tells your hindbrain a very large bear is nearby. Then it comes through the undergrowth like a landslide with feathers, and the two sounds become one creature: eight feet of muscle, beak, claws, and bad intentions, moving with the spe...

name: Pegasus
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — pure; the cleanest, most joyful air-signature in Oreka, flight as an expression of freedom
environment: Eternal Steppe (the open grasslands — particularly the high-steppe regions where the horizon is unbroken and the wind runs unimpeded, far from the forest-edge and far from mountains, in the sky above the steppe’s heart)
description: The herd moves across the steppe-sky at dawn — a dozen winged horses silhouetted against the pink-gold horizon, their flight-formation as precise as any Mytroan cavalry column but achieved without riders, without commands, without anything except the collective grace of creatures for whom the sky is home. They are white, or silver, or pale gold, and they fly with a wingspan that should not support a horse-body but does, because the wind beneath them is not entirely wind. Something else holds ...

name: Peri
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire — sacred; not the destructive fire of the Scorchspires but the illuminating, purifying fire of dawn, the fire that reveals truth and burns only corruption
environment: Glass Wastes / Tharok’s Pillar (the heat-blasted ruins of the Infinite Desert’s most ancient Giant cities — particularly the sacred sites where the Lady of Fire’s influence is strongest, where the sand has been fused to glass by heat so old it predates memory)
description: The Glass Wastes shimmer at midday, heat-haze turning the ruined columns of Tharok’s Pillar into dancing shapes that could be anything — pilgrims, mirage-beasts, or the melting edges of the world itself. And then one of the shimmering shapes steps out of the haze, and it is neither mirage nor heat-dream. It is a woman made of fire that does not burn — golden-skinned, amber-eyed, with wings of flame that fold and unfold like a heartbeat. The air around her is warm, not hot. The light she produ...

name: Phase Spider
flags: elemental_affinity:None — between; phase spiders exist partially outside the elemental framework, their resonance flickering between present and absent as they shift between planes
environment: Gatefall Reach Forests (the mixed deciduous forests of the Gatefall Reach — particularly the border-zone near the Silence Breach where the planar membrane is thinnest and phase-shifting is easiest)
description: The web is invisible. Not hidden — invisible. The spider spins its web on the Ethereal Plane and waits there, watching the Material Plane through the membrane between worlds, until a creature walks into the space that corresponds to the web’s location. Then it shifts — phases — from the Ethereal to the Material, materialising directly behind its prey with fangs already extended. The attack lasts less than a second. Then the spider phases back, taking its venom and its victim’s screams with it...

name: Phaya Nak
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — deep, ancient; the pressure and darkness of the deepest ocean, the water that has never seen sunlight, old beyond reckoning
environment: Deep Apelian Sea (the abyssal trenches of the eastern Apelian continental shelf — lightless, pressurised depths that no Kin diver can reach, surfacing only rarely along the Tidebloom and Deepwater Marches coastlines)
description: The Apelian Sea is deep in places that even the Na’wasua do not map. There are trenches along the eastern continental shelf that descend beyond light, beyond pressure, beyond the reach of any diving magic that Kin have devised. The halfling deep-sea fishers of the Tidebloom coast know these places — not because they go there, but because sometimes something comes up from them. A shape in the water, so vast it darkens the surface from below like a cloud passing in front of the sun, except the ...

name: Phi Krahang
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — corrupted marsh; the stagnant water of a swamp that has curdled, the marsh at its worst, decay given predatory purpose
environment: Deepwater Marshes (the outer edges of the Deepwater Marches — the deep-marsh zones where mangrove gives way to open swamp, far from halfling settlements, active at night)
description: The marsh-lights are wrong tonight. The halfling fishers of the outer Deepwater Marches know their marsh — they know the will-o’-wisps, the phosphorescent fungi, the bioluminescent insects that make the swamp glow after dark. These lights are different. They move against the wind, in formation, and they are approaching the fishing-platforms at a speed that no natural light-source produces. Then the figure emerges from the tree-line — a man, or something shaped like a man, riding two rice-winn...

name: Phi Pop
flags: elemental_affinity:None — undead; a parasitic undead resonance that masks itself beneath the ambient signal of its host community
environment: Deepwater Marches (halfling settlements and their surrounding marsh-communities — not in the deep swamp but in the settlements, hiding among the living)
description: The halfling village is dying. Not suddenly — slowly, from the inside, the way a tree dies when something is eating its roots. The strongest men sicken. The healthiest children pale and thin. The healers find nothing — no disease, no poison, no curse that their remedies can identify. But at night, when the village sleeps, something moves through the houses. Not through the doors — through the walls, through the floors, through the spaces between. It feeds while they sleep, and what it feeds o...

name: Phi Tai Hong
flags: elemental_affinity:None — violent death imprint; a phi tai hong is the psychic scar of a violent or unjust death burned into a location, grief and rage so concentrated it persists after the body is gone
alignment: Always chaotic evil (the original person’s alignment is irrelevant — the phi tai hong is pure concentrated anguish, not a continuation of the deceased’s personality)
environment: Great River Gorge (the narrow cliff-walled sections of the Great River where the water cuts through ancient stone — particularly the deep gorges between Pekakarlik river-towns where the current is swift, the walls are high, and deaths by drowning, falling, or ambush are most common)
organization: Solitary (always — each phi tai hong is a unique death)
advancement: 7–10 HD (Medium); 11–14 HD (Large — phi tai hong fed by additional violent deaths at the same site)
description: The gorge narrows where the Great River cuts through the old rock, and in the narrowing the air changes. Not temperature — something else. The Pekakarlik boatman has gone quiet. His pole rests against the gunwale, untouched, and the barge drifts on the current alone. He is watching the cliff-face, specifically the ledge thirty feet above the waterline where the wildflowers grow too thick and too bright for a place that gets no sunlight. “Someone died there,” he says. “Badly.” You look again. ...

name: Piasa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) — riverine; the resonance of river-clay, deep-bank mud, and the slow patient erosion of stone by water over centuries
environment: Steppe-River Border (the river-crossings, fords, and bluff-lines where the Great River and its tributaries cut through the Eternal Steppe’s edge — specifically the transitional zone where open grassland meets river-valley, where travellers are funnelled through narrow crossing-points)
organization: Solitary (always — piasa are aggressively territorial against their own kind)
description: The thing above the river is not a bird. You knew that before your eyes confirmed it — the wingbeats are wrong, too heavy, displaced air moving in a pattern that no bird produces. It is a dragon, but not like the dragons the scholars describe: no chromatic scales, no metallic sheen, no alignment to the elemental framework your tutors taught you. It is the colour of river-clay and old blood, its wings are feathered like an eagle’s rather than membraned like a drake’s, its face carries antlers ...

name: Pishtaco
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — dead cold; mountain-earth drained of warmth, the resonance of stone that should be alive with geological heat but has been hollowed out, leaving only cold mass
environment: Mountain Passes (the high passes, narrow defiles, and single-track trails of the Giant’s Teeth and surrounding ranges — specifically the remote, infrequently-travelled passes between Orean foothill communities where travellers are isolated and rescue is days away)
advancement: 8–10 HD (Medium); 11–14 HD (Large — pishtaco that have fed extensively grow bloated and swollen)
description: The mountain pass smells wrong. You have been climbing for six hours through good clean air — pine-resin, cold stone, the metallic scent of altitude — and now something has changed. It smells like a butcher’s shop on a warm day: blood and fat and the sweetish tang of meat beginning to turn. The Orean guide has stopped walking. He is looking at the path ahead, where the rocks narrow to a single-file passage between two cliff-faces, and he is looking at the ground, where something glistens in t...

name: Pixie
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — mischievous; the lightest, most playful expression of air-resonance, wind as laughter, breeze as practical joke
environment: Great Forests (the mid-canopy and understory of Oreka’s oldest forest zones — particularly the groves where sunlight filters through the canopy in dappled patterns, creating the shifting light-and-shadow that pixies prefer; common in Tanuki groves, Pasua patrol-ranges, and the forest-edges bordering the Eternal Steppe)
description: You hear the laughter before you see anything — high, silvery, coming from somewhere above your left ear and slightly to the right and also behind you and possibly from inside the hollow log you just stepped over. Then a mote of light drifts past your face, and you feel a tug at your belt-pouch, and when you look down, your coins are gone. When you look up, a creature the size of your hand is sitting on a branch at eye-level, counting your money with an expression of delighted concentration. ...

name: Poludnitsa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) — noon-blaze; the killing heat of midday sun, fire expressed not as flame but as radiance so intense it stops the heart, the Lady of Fire’s destructive aspect turned to punishment
environment: Volcanic Lowlands (the cultivated fields, terrace-farms, and open ground of Rarozhki agricultural territory in the Scorchspire lowlands — specifically the exposed, sun-baked areas where field-workers labour during the growing season and where noon-heat is most intense)
description: Noon in the volcanic lowlands is a thing that has weight. The sun does not simply shine — it presses, flattens, bakes the ground until the air above the dark soil shimmers and bends. Nothing moves at noon. The Rarozhki field-workers have retreated to the shade-shelters, the goats stand motionless under the rock-overhangs, and even the insects have gone quiet. Into this silence, something walks. A woman, tall, dressed in white, moving across the open field with a step that leaves no mark on th...

name: Ponaturi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — drowned dark; water in enclosed spaces, water that has not seen sunlight, the cold black water that fills tombs and basements and the spaces beneath the world
environment: Flooded Giant Ruins (the submerged and partially-submerged Giant-era structures along the Kovaka coast and in the lowland forests where rising water-tables have flooded ancient architecture — specifically the great columned halls, step-pyramids, and underground cisterns that the Giants built before the Kin and that now sit beneath metres of dark water)
description: The flooded ruin is deeper than it looks. The Giant-era columns rise from water that should be waist-deep but isn’t — the floor drops away somewhere between the entrance and the first colonnade, and by the time you realise you’re swimming rather than wading, the shapes are already rising from below. They are humanoid, pale as drowned flesh, with wide flat eyes that reflect your torchlight back at you with a greenish luminescence. They are smiling. Every single one of them is smiling — not the...

name: Popobawa
flags: elemental_affinity:None — shadow outsider; the popobawa exists in the space between elemental resonances, a predator of the gap, registering on Kin-sense as concentrated dread — not absence (like Dómnathar void) but active malice, the feeling of being watched by something that feeds on fear
environment: Scorchspire Settlements (the stone-built villages, farming-terraces, and valley-floor communities of Taraf-Imro territory in the Scorchspire foothills — specifically the isolated settlements at volcanic-valley edges where darkness is absolute at night and where the gap between houses creates shadows deep enough for a predator to hide)
organization: Solitary (always — popobawa are aggressively solitary and territorial)
description: The settlement goes quiet after dark — not the comfortable quiet of a village at rest, but the held-breath quiet of prey that knows the predator is already inside the pen. The Taraf-Imro warrior on the roof has not moved in twenty minutes. He is watching the shadows between the stone houses, watching for the thing that does not have a fixed shape, that moves through walls without opening doors, that smells of sulphur and bat-musk and something worse — the sweet-rot stink of a creature that fe...

name: Púca
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) — crossroads; the resonance of the place where road meets road, where earth and air mix at the boundary between tame and wild, cultivated and untamed, known and unknown
environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the crossroads, forest-edges, river-fords, and boundary-points where the Great Forests meet the Eternal Steppe — specifically the transitional zones where no single Kin culture claims authority and where the rules of civilisation begin to fray)
advancement: 6–8 HD (Large); 9–12 HD (Huge — rare, very old specimens)
description: The horse is standing at the crossroads where the forest meets the steppe, and it is the most beautiful horse you have ever seen — midnight-black, tall, with eyes that gleam like copper coins in the last light of day. It is watching you with an intelligence that horses do not possess, and its ears are pricked forward with an expression you would describe as amused. The Pasua ranger beside you says, very quietly, “Don’t get on.” You were not planning to get on. But now that she’s said it, you ...

name: Purple Worm
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — tectonic; the resonance of geological movement, of continental plates grinding against each other, of the earth itself shifting — not the quiet patience of mountain-stone but the active, destructive energy of the earth in motion
environment: Deep Desert (the Infinite Desert’s deepest hardpan zones, the substrata beneath the dune-fields, and the buried rock-layers that underlie the Glass Wastes — the worm moves through the desert’s bones, not its surface)
description: The desert does not move. That is the first thing every Pekakarlik caravan-guide learns and the last thing every dead caravan-guide forgot. The desert does not move — and if it does, if the dunes shift when there is no wind, if the hardpan cracks along a line that is too straight and too long to be natural, if the ground beneath your feet begins to vibrate with a frequency that your bones recognise before your brain does, then you have exactly the amount of time it takes to scream before the ...

name: Qareen
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — personal; the resonance of one person’s relationship with the sea, intimate and specific, like the sound of a wave that has only ever broken on one stretch of shore
alignment: Usually neutral (matches bonded partner’s alignment tendency)
environment: Na’wasua Groves (the tidal grove-settlements, sea-caves, and reef-shelters of Na’wasua territory along the Apelian coast — specifically the places where Na’wasua community life happens, where the boundary between land and sea is most permeable and the Lady of the Sea’s presence is most intimate)
advancement: 5–8 HD (Small); 9–12 HD (Medium — qareen whose bonded partner reaches great age or spiritual depth)
description: The Na’wasua diver does not swim alone. You understand this intellectually — you have been told this, multiple times, by people who know — but you do not believe it until you see it. She dives from the canoe into water that is twenty fathoms deep and very dark, and as she descends, something descends with her. It is not visible, not exactly — more like a shimmer in the water where a shimmer has no business being, a shape that the current defines by flowing around rather than through. It moves...

name: Qilin
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords — unified; the only creature in Oreka whose resonance carries all four elemental signatures in perfect harmony, not blended or mixed but complete, as though the world itself were looking through a single pair of eyes
environment: Universal (a qilin appears wherever and whenever the world determines it must — there is no habitat, no territory, no range; a qilin has been sighted once in the Infinite Desert, once above the Apelian Sea, once in the deep Vestri mines, and once on the Eternal Steppe, and each sighting occurred at a moment of profound significance)
organization: Solitary (if “solitary” can describe something that may be the only one of its kind)
description: You do not encounter the qilin. The qilin encounters you — or more precisely, the qilin encounters the moment, because a qilin does not appear for people. It appears for events. You are standing at a crossroads, or on a battlefield the morning after, or in a council-chamber where a decision is about to be made that will change things, and the air changes. Not the temperature. The quality. The light becomes kinder. The wind becomes gentler. The sounds of the world — birds, insects, distant voi...

name: Rakshasa
flags: elemental_affinity:None — false; a rakshasa generates a convincing but artificial elemental resonance that matches whatever identity it is currently wearing, constructed through sheer force of will and centuries of practice; the false resonance is detectable as fake only on a DC 25 Kin-sense check
environment: Warm Settlements (the river-cities of the Eruskan, the oasis-towns of the Pekakarlik, the larger trading-posts of the Kinsweave border-kingdoms — anywhere commerce creates wealth, complexity creates cover, and population density creates prey)
description: The merchant is perfect. His smile is warm, his voice is cultured, his robes are exactly the quality a man of his station would wear — not ostentatious, not modest, the precise median. His hands are soft and well-manicured. His knowledge of the spice-trade is encyclopaedic. He has been a fixture of this Eruskan river-city for eleven years, and every person who knows him considers him reliable, generous, and utterly trustworthy. The only thing wrong with him — the only thing — is that his hand...

name: Rast
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) — vent-heat; the raw, undirected thermal energy of volcanic fissures, fire as geological process rather than element, heat without light
environment: Scorchspires (the volcanic vents, fumaroles, and sulphur-fields of the Scorchspire range — specifically the active geothermal zones where the boundary between the Material Plane and the Elemental Plane of Fire is thinnest)
description: The Scorchspire vent is breathing. Not metaphorically — the sulphurous air pulses in and out of the fissure with a rhythm that is too regular to be geological. Something is in there, something that breathes fire the way you breathe air, and when it emerges — crawling out of the crack on too many legs, its body the colour of cooling magma, its mouth already open and already glowing — you realise that the heat you felt from twenty feet away is not the vent. It is the creature. The vent is merel...

name: Remorhaz
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) — subsurface inferno; the resonance of superheated rock, of magma channels too deep for even the Rarozhki to mine, of the earth’s internal fire expressed through a living body
environment: Infinite Desert Hardpan (the baked, compressed substrata beneath the Infinite Desert’s dune-fields — the hardpan layer that lies between the surface sand and the deep rock, where temperatures are extreme and the geological pressure creates conditions that no surface creature can tolerate)
description: The hardpan cracks. Not like drought-cracking — like something pressing up from beneath, a line of fractures racing across the baked earth faster than a horse can run. The Pekakarlik scout is already moving, already running, already screaming the word that every desert-traveller learns before any other: “Below!” The ground splits open along the fracture-line and something erupts — something enormous, segmented, centipede-shaped, its body radiating heat so intense that the air above it shimmer...

name: River Serpent
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — warden; the resonance of the living river, water that is healthy and watched and protected, the Lady of the Sea’s freshwater domain maintained through her eldest servants
environment: Great River (the full length of the Great River and its major tributaries — from the headwaters in the Giant’s Teeth foothills through the Eruskan river-cities to the delta where the Great River meets the Apelian Sea; also present in the Twin Rivers region and Kinsweave lake systems)
advancement: 6–10 HD (Large); 11–15 HD (Huge — transition to Great Serpent)
description: The Great River is deep here — deep enough that you cannot see the bottom from the barge’s railing, deep enough that the Pekakarlik boatman navigates by current-reading and memory rather than by sight. And then, in the deep green below, something moves. It is long — longer than the barge — and it moves with the effortless sinuosity of a creature for which the river is not habitat but home in the most fundamental sense. You catch a flash of iridescent scale, blue-green and silver, and a head t...

name: Roc
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — sovereign; the most massive expression of air-resonance in Oreka’s living creatures, wind given the weight and authority of a mountain, the sky’s own apex predator
environment: Mountain Peaks (the highest peaks of the Giant’s Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Ashen Crown — above the snow-line, above the cloud-line, in the thin air where nothing else flies and the wind is the only law)
description: The shadow passes over the caravan and everything goes dark — not like a cloud, because clouds don’t move that fast and don’t have that shape. You look up. The wingspan blots out the sun. It is a bird — your brain insists it is a bird, because it has wings and feathers and a beak — but it is a bird the way a mountain is a hill. It is the size of a ship. Its talons, each one longer than a man is tall, are open and reaching, and the thing it is reaching for is the lead pack-elephant, which weig...

name: Roper
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — living stone; the resonance of mineral deposits that have developed awareness, stone that has been stone so long it has learned to hunger
environment: Deep Caverns (the deepest natural cave-systems of the Giant’s Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Ashen Crown — well below Vestri mining-depth, in the eternal-dark zones where no light has ever reached and the only sounds are the drip of groundwater and the occasional groan of settling rock)
description: The stalagmite has an eye. You do not see it immediately — you have been walking through the cavern for twenty minutes, and the stalagmites here are tall and thick and densely packed, and your attention is on the ground (uneven, slick, treacherous). But the Vestri miner leading you has stopped, and he is looking at a specific stalagmite with an expression that has gone very still, very controlled, and when you follow his gaze you see it: a single yellow eye, lidless and unblinking, set in the...

name: Rust Monster
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — corroded; the resonance of oxidation, of metal returning to ore, of the earth reclaiming what was taken from it — entropy given form and appetite
environment: Vestri Mining Tunnels (the active and abandoned mine-shafts, ore-processing tunnels, and metal-storage facilities of the Vestri Dwarven mining complex in the Giant’s Teeth — specifically wherever metal is mined, stored, transported, or worked)
description: The thing is about the size of a large dog, with a body like an armadillo’s, antennae that wave independently of each other like a dowser’s rods, and a long, flat tail that it uses for balance as it scurries toward you with a speed that is frankly alarming for a creature of its build. But you are not alarmed by the creature itself. You are alarmed by the expression on the Vestri miner’s face — the miner who has faced olgoi-khorkhoi and cave-spiders and once punched a grick — because the miner...

name: Şahmeran
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — deep-tunnel ancient; the resonance of stone that has been stone since the world began, undisturbed, unmined, unknown — earth so deep and so old that it predates even the Lord of Stone’s awareness of it
environment: Deep Tunnels (the deepest tunnel-systems beneath the Giant’s Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Kinsweave — below Vestri mining-depth, below Dómnathar operational zones, in the primordial rock-chambers where nothing has walked since the world formed; şahmeran inhabit sealed chambers that they have occupied for millennia)
description: The tunnel opens into a chamber that the Vestri deep-shaft maps do not show — unmarked, unmined, sealed behind a wall that someone built from the other side. The wall is old. The chamber beyond it is older. The air is warm and still and carries a scent that is not sulphur, not stone-dust, not the metallic tang of deep rock — it is sweet, like honey left in the sun, and underneath the sweetness there is something else, something that your hindbrain recognises before your conscious mind does: v...

name: Salamander, Average
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) — forge-bound; fire with purpose, heat directed toward creation, the Lady of Fire’s domain expressed as the transformative power of the forge
environment: Scorchspire Vents (the deepest volcanic vents and magma-channels of the Scorchspire range, where the Material Plane and the Elemental Plane of Fire overlap — specifically the forge-vent complexes where salamander colonies maintain permanent workshops)
description: The heat hits you like a wall — not the dry heat of desert air but the wet, aggressive heat of a forge-room with the bellows running. The vent ahead glows orange, and something is moving in the glow: a creature whose upper body is roughly humanoid — muscular, dark-skinned, with eyes like embers — and whose lower body is a coiled serpent-trunk of living fire, leaving scorched stone in its wake. It carries a spear that glows white-hot, and it is looking at you the way a blacksmith looks at unwo...

name: Salamander, Noble
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) — sovereign forge; fire at its most authoritative, the Lady of Fire’s creative aspect expressed as mastery over heat and metal, the forge elevated to absolute command
environment: Ember Vault (the deepest, hottest forge-chambers of the Scorchspires’ volcanic core — permanent colonies established at the boundary between the Material Plane and the Elemental Plane of Fire)
organization: Solitary, pair, or noble court (1 noble + 3–10 average salamanders)
description: Large Outsider (Fire, Extraplanar)

name: Salt Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — crystalline desiccation; the resonance of salt-deposits, of water removed from earth until only the mineral skeleton remains, earth at its driest and most hostile to life
environment: Desert Salt Flats (the vast salt-pan expanses of the Infinite Desert — specifically the dried lake-beds and mineral-flat zones where ancient water-sources evaporated millennia ago, leaving crystalline wastelands)
description: The salt flat shimmers in the heat. The crust is thin here — thin enough that your boot punches through with each step, revealing the damp brine beneath. Something crunches to your left, and a shape rises from the salt-crust like a man rising from shallow water: small, angular, encrusted with crystalline deposits, with a face that is all sharp edges and a grin that contains too many teeth. It smells like a low-tide beach on a hot day. It is looking at your waterskin with the focused intensity...

name: Sand Wraith
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords (fragmented); strongest resonance with the Lord of Stone — a Sand Wraith’s elemental signature is the shattered remnant of a Giant’s full four-element harmony, broken and looping like a record stuck in a groove
special_qualities: elemental resonance (all four - fragmented Giant echo)
environment: The Infinite Desert (Giant ruins, dune-seas; thickest concentration at the Sunken Thrones, Tharok’s Pillar, and the deep dune-seas; rare sightings at the Apelian Sea’s submerged Giant piers in the Deepwater Marches)
description: The ruin ahead is half-buried — the upper columns of what was once a Giant plaza project from the dune like the ribs of a vast skeleton, and the sand between them moves in patterns that have nothing to do with the wind. A shape gathers in the air above the columns: tall, vaguely humanoid, composed entirely of swirling sand and grit. Dim amber light burns where its eyes should be. The sound it produces is less a voice than a sustained, low howl of desert wind forced through a space too narrow ...

name: Satyr
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Youngest Brother (Earth/Air) — forest-edge; the resonance of where cultivated land meets wildwood, earth-under-leaves meeting wind-through-branches, the boundary between the tamed and the untamed expressed as music
environment: Great Forests (the mid-forest zones between deep canopy and forest-edge — specifically the clearings, stream-banks, and meadow-openings where sunlight reaches the ground and the forest is at its most welcoming and most deceptive)
description: The music reaches you before the musician does. It is coming from deep in the forest — a pipe-melody that winds between the trees like smoke, changing key with each turn of the path, and you find yourself walking toward it without having decided to. The undergrowth parts. The clearing opens. He is sitting on a fallen oak, legs crossed — the legs of a goat, furred and hooved, below the bare-chested torso of a man who looks like he has never done anything more strenuous than smile. He is playin...

name: Sea Cat
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — coastal; the resonance of shallow-water hunting-grounds, of the turbulent zone where wave meets reef and the sea’s predatory energy is most concentrated
environment: Apelian Sea (the coastal shallows, reef-systems, and island-chains of the Apelian basin — specifically the warm-water zones where fish-stocks are dense enough to support a large apex predator; secondary presence along the Kailian coast)
description: The Apelian shallows are not empty — you knew this — but you were watching for serpents and sharks, and the thing that surfaces thirty feet from the boat is neither. It has the forelimbs, mane, and head of a great lion, and the hind-body and tail of a fish, and it moves through the water with a power and grace that a lion on land cannot match. Its roar carries across the water — not the deep bass of a terrestrial lion but a sharper, higher sound, pitched to carry over waves — and the fishing-...

name: Sea Hag
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — drowned malice; the resonance of water as instrument of death, not the Lady’s nurturing aspect but her cold, drowning, killing aspect — the sea that takes
environment: Kailian Coast (the rocky, tide-battered stretches of coastline where the Kailian meets the Apelian basin — sea-caves, tidal pools, half-drowned grottoes, and the fog-shrouded coves where ships run aground and survivors wash ashore into worse)
organization: Solitary or coven (3 hags of any kind)
description: The woman on the rock is not beautiful, though the quality of her ugliness has a kind of terrible fascination. She is hunched, dripping, with kelp-tangled hair and skin the colour of a drowned corpse. Her eyes are the worst part — they are aware, they are watching you, and behind the milky surface there is a cruel intelligence that is evaluating whether you are worth the effort. She opens her mouth. Her teeth are black. “Come closer,” she says, and her voice is the sound of waves pulling a bo...

name: Shadow
