flags: elemental_affinity:None — Dómnathar void; a shadow registers on Kin-sense as active absence, the characteristic void-signature of the Dómnathar ecosystem — not the natural emptiness of a dead zone but the hungry, purposeful nothing of the False Silence
environment: Dómnathar Zones (the periphery of Dómnathar tunnel-networks, Silence Breach zones, abandoned Dómnathar outposts, and any location where the False Silence has left a permanent residue — shadows are the undead by-product of Dómnathar presence, forming where elemental resonance has been suppressed long enough for the void itself to develop hunger)
description: The torch dies. Not gutters, not flickers — dies, snuffed as completely as if it had been plunged into water. The darkness that follows is not normal darkness. It has weight. It has direction. It moves toward you, and where it touches the wall, the stone loses its colour — grey bleaching to white, moisture evaporating, warmth draining away. Something is in the dark, and it is not absence. It is presence — the presence of nothing, the active malice of a void that is hungry.

name: Shambling Mound
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Lady of the Sea (Earth/Water) — marsh-growth; the resonance of waterlogged earth producing uncontrolled growth, the border-zone where soil meets water and everything that falls in is consumed and converted to more growth
environment: Forest-Marsh Border (the transitional wetlands between the Great Forests and the Deepwater Marches — specifically the zones where standing water meets forest undergrowth, creating the dense, decaying vegetation that shambling mounds require)
description: The marsh-edge is dense with vegetation — cattails, sedge-grass, moss-draped willows — and the shapes in the undergrowth are the shapes of rotting logs and mounded compost. You have been walking past them for ten minutes. One of them stands up. It is fifteen feet tall, roughly humanoid, composed entirely of rotting vegetation — vines and roots and decaying plant-matter compressed into a body that smells of compost and moves with a slow, deliberate power that belies its shambling gait. It has ...

name: Shedim
flags: elemental_affinity:None — liminal; shedim exist in the twilight between elemental signatures, in the perceptual gaps where Kin-sense transitions between readings and cannot resolve clearly
environment: Twilight Zones (the liminal spaces at the edges of goblin settlements — the grey areas between day and night, between forest and clearing, between the wild static of goblin communities and the blank absence of uninhabited wilderness; shedim manifest where the world is between states)
description: The twilight is wrong. You have walked through twilight a hundred times — the grey hour between day and night when the world is neither lit nor dark, when shadows have no source and distance becomes unreliable. This twilight does not end. It has been grey for twenty minutes, and the sun is not setting and has not risen, and the goblin settlement ahead is quiet in a way that goblin settlements are never quiet. Something stands in the gap between the last house and the treeline — a shape that i...

name: Shocker Lizard
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — bioelectric; the resonance of living electricity, the air-element expressed through biological discharge rather than atmospheric phenomena
environment: Deepwater Marches (the warm freshwater marshes, swamp-pools, and reed-beds of the Deepwater Marches — specifically the shallow, still-water zones where shocker lizard colonies can establish overlapping electrical fields)
description: The marsh-pool ahead is shallow and murky, and the thing sitting on the half-submerged log is about the size of a house cat — blue-grey, smooth-skinned, with a wide flat head and eyes that track your movement with reptilian precision. It looks harmless. Then the air crackles, your arm-hairs stand up, and the log lights up — blue-white sparks arcing between the lizard’s flanks and the water’s surface. The second lizard surfaces to your left. The third is behind you. The water between them begi...

name: Si’Lāt
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — false intimacy; a si’lāt generates water-resonance that mimics the personal, intimate quality of a qareen bond, creating a false sense of trust and familiarity that exploits the Na’wasua’s deepest emotional connections
environment: Na’wasua Groves (the tidal grove-settlements, reef-caves, and lagoon-shelters of Na’wasua territory — specifically the locations where Na’wasua community life is most intimate and where the boundary between welcomed guest and infiltrating predator is thinnest)
description: The Na’wasua grove is quiet — the natural quiet of a reef-sheltered lagoon at low tide. The woman walking through the shallows ahead of you is beautiful in the way that Na’wasua sea-elves are beautiful — long-limbed, dark-skinned, silver-haired, moving through the water with the unconscious grace of someone born to it. She turns. She smiles. And then your qareen — the companion-spirit you have had since birth, the water-presence that has never left your side — goes cold. Not distant. Cold. Th...

name: Simurgh
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords — harmonised; the simurgh carries all four elemental signatures in deliberate, conscious balance — not the absolute unity of the qilin (which is an event) but the studied, wise harmony of a creature that has learned to hold all four elements in equilibrium through millennia of existence
environment: Giant’s Teeth Peaks (the highest, most remote peaks of the Giant’s Teeth — above the roc nesting-zones, above the ccoa territories, in the thin air where only the wind and the simurgh remain)
description: The mountain is silent — the kind of silence that exists only above the snow-line, where the wind has scoured the rock clean of everything that could make a sound. And then the silence breaks, not with noise but with presence. Something is above you — something vast, slow-moving, and radiating an awareness that feels like standing in sunlight after years indoors. You look up. The wingspan covers the sky. It is a bird — or it was a bird, once, before it became something else: feathered in bron...

name: Skeleton
flags: elemental_affinity:None — skeletons are animated objects, not elemental beings; they have no resonance, no signature, and no connection to the elemental system — they are bones made to move by magic, nothing more
environment: Any (wherever the dead are raised — battlefields, crypts, Dómnathar tunnels, necromantic workshops, cursed sites where ambient death-energy animates remains without a spellcaster’s intervention)
description: Medium Undead

name: Skinwalker
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Youngest Brother (Earth/Air) — stolen, shifting; a skinwalker’s resonance is not its own — it is a corrupted fragment of whatever creature’s form it has most recently worn, earth and air twisted into something that mimics life without possessing it
environment: Eternal Steppe (the open grasslands, river-valleys, and camp-grounds of Mytroan territory — specifically the edges of Mytroan settlements and herding-routes, where the steppe meets the dark and the boundary between human space and wild space is thinnest)
description: The Mytroan camp-fire is burning low, and the herder on watch is the last one awake. The horse at the picket-line is nervous — stamping, pulling at its tether, nostrils flared. The herder watches the horse and does not watch the darkness behind him, and that is the mistake. Something comes out of the steppe on four legs — a wolf, large and dark, moving with a wolf’s easy lope — and then it stops, and the wolf unfolds. The hind-legs straighten. The forelegs become arms. The spine elongates. Th...

name: Spectre
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) — extinguished; the resonance of fire that has gone out, heat that has been removed, the Lady of Fire’s domain inverted into its opposite — not cold (which is the absence of heat) but the memory of heat, the agonising awareness of warmth that no longer exists
environment: Ashen Hollows (the dead volcanic valleys of the Scorchspire foothills — places where volcanic activity ceased, where the Lady of Fire’s presence once burned and now does not, leaving a residue of elemental grief that produces the most powerful incorporeal undead in the region)
description: The Ashen Hollows are the places where fire died — the volcanic valleys of the Scorchspire foothills where eruptions once poured lava and now pour nothing, where the ground is cold and grey and the trees are petrified stumps. Nothing lives here. That is the first sign that something dead does. The shape that rises from the grey earth is not smoke — it is darker than smoke, more defined, and it moves with the deliberate purpose of something that remembers being alive and hates you for still be...

name: Spider Eater
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — canopy-predator; the resonance of the Great Forest’s upper airspace, wind filtered through leaves and channelled between tree-trunks
environment: Great Forests (the mid-canopy and upper-canopy zones of the Great Forests — specifically the zones where giant spider populations are dense enough to support spider eater breeding cycles)
description: The thing drops from the canopy — thirty feet of membranous wing spanning between a body like a wasp’s, a head like a horse’s, and a stinger the length of your forearm. It is not falling. It is diving, and the target is not you but the giant spider in the web between the oaks. The stinger drives into the spider’s abdomen with surgical precision, deposits something, and the creature is airborne again before the spider can react. In three days, the spider’s body will burst open. The larva insid...

name: Spirit Naga
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — corrupted; the resonance of water that has been claimed and twisted by an intelligence that uses the Lady’s domain for its own purposes — river-current directed by will rather than gravity, water that serves a master
environment: Corrupted Waterways (stretches of river, creek, or lake where a spirit naga has established territorial dominance — the water itself changes, becoming sluggish, discoloured, and hostile to normal aquatic life; most commonly found along Great River tributaries and the smaller waterways of the Kinsweave and Deepwater Marches)
description: The waterway ahead is wrong. The current runs sluggishly, the colour is off — a faint yellow-green tinge beneath the surface — and the reeds along the bank are wilted despite adequate water. Something has corrupted this stretch of river, and the corruption has a voice. It speaks from the water: a woman’s voice, cultured, persuasive, and utterly, chillingly reasonable, explaining why you should come closer, why you should listen, why resistance to the inevitable is wasteful. The voice belongs ...

name: Stirge
flags: elemental_affinity:None — stirges have no elemental resonance; they are blood-feeding parasites that register on Kin-sense as barely-there biological noise, too small and too simple to carry elemental identity
environment: Deepwater Marches (the warm, humid marshlands and swamp-forests of the Deepwater Marches — specifically the stagnant pools, reed-beds, and dense undergrowth where stirge swarms breed in standing water)
description: The first one lands on your neck before you feel it. That is the thing about stirges — they are small, they are quiet, and they latch on before you know they are there. The proboscis is thin as a needle and slides into your skin with a precision that a surgeon would envy. By the time you slap at it, the first pint is already gone. The second stirge lands on your arm. The third is on the horse. There are more in the air — a dozen, two dozen, darting and hovering like oversized mosquitoes with ...

name: Stone Giant
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — Giant remnant; the resonance of the original Giants’ earth-attunement, diminished and fragmented but recognisably derived from the same source — the Lord of Stone’s domain expressed through beings that carry the Giants’ bloodline in diluted, feral form
environment: Giant’s Teeth (the high mountain valleys, cliff-dwellings, and cave-systems of the Giant’s Teeth range — specifically the zones above Vestri mining-depth and below the snow-line, in the rock-country where stone giants can use their stone-throwing and rock-catching abilities to maximum tactical advantage)
organization: Solitary, gang (2–5), band (6–9 plus 35% noncombatants), or tribe (21–30 plus 50% noncombatants)
description: The boulder moves. Not falls — moves, with the deliberate precision of something thrown by hands that know exactly where it will land. The second boulder follows three seconds later, and it lands exactly where the first would have driven you if you’d run. You look up. The figure on the cliff is sixty feet away and thirty feet tall, grey-skinned, long-armed, with a face like weathered granite and eyes that are calculating the trajectory of the third rock.

name: Storm Wisp
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — raw; the purest, most unrefined expression of the Youngest Brother’s domain, wind as playful electrical discharge, the air-element at its most mischievous and least controlled
environment: Great Forests (primary — the forest canopy and clearings during storm season) and Eternal Steppe (secondary — open grasslands during thunderstorms); anywhere with frequent storms and sustained wind
advancement: 4–6 HD (Tiny); 7–9 HD (Small — transition toward Storm Herald)
description: The air crackles. A blue-white sphere of light — no larger than a fist, but bright enough to leave afterimages — darts between the trees, pauses, darts again. It moves like a dragonfly: sudden direction-changes, instantaneous stops, bursts of speed that defy any creature with mass. Your arm-hair stands up. The taste of metal fills your mouth. The Pasua scout beside you grins. “Wisp,” she says. “Don’t touch. Don’t touch anything metal.”

name: Supay
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — sovereign depth; the resonance of the deepest earth, the stone that has never seen light, the Lord of Stone’s domain at its most absolute and most ancient — not the worked-stone of mines or the living-stone of mountains but the primordial substrate beneath everything
environment: Deep Caverns (the deepest natural cave-systems beneath the Giant’s Teeth foothills — specifically the ancient mine-shafts and subterranean chambers that Orean mountain-communities have mapped and sealed, where the earth is old enough to have its own ruler)
organization: Solitary (always — a supay claims a domain and rules it alone)
description: The mine-shaft is old — older than the Vestri tunnels above it, older than the Orean settlements in the foothills, old enough that the tool-marks on the walls were made by hands larger than any Kin’s. The air at the bottom of the shaft is not stale. It is warm, the wrong kind of warm — not geothermal warmth but the warmth of a presence, of something breathing in the dark. The Orean miners who mapped this shaft went no further than this landing, and the reason is carved into the wall in Old Or...

name: Svartálfar Shade
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — fading; the resonance of Vestri earth-attunement diminished by death but not extinguished — a candle’s afterglow where a forge-fire once burned, recognisably dwarven, recognisably diminished
environment: Abandoned Dwarf Halls (the sealed, evacuated, or forgotten mining-halls and forge-complexes of the Vestri Dwarven infrastructure — specifically the halls abandoned during the centuries of contraction following the Fall of Aldenheim, where Vestri dead were left in memorial-chambers when the living withdrew)
description: The dwarf-hall is empty. It has been empty for a long time — the forge-fires are cold, the tool-racks bare, the sleeping-quarters stripped of everything portable. The Vestri who abandoned this hall took everything they could carry and left everything they could not, and what they could not carry included their dead. The shade drifts through the doorway of the memorial-chamber with the slow, deliberate movement of something that is still performing a duty it was assigned a very long time ago. ...

name: Taniwha
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea / Lord of Stone (Water/Earth) — cave-pool sovereign; the resonance of underground water in shaped stone, the Lady’s domain enclosed by the Lord’s architecture — water that has been held in stone so long that the two elements have merged into something inseparable
environment: Cave Pools (the deep, still pools within Kovaka coastal cave-systems — specifically the limestone caverns and sea-caves where underground freshwater meets tidal salt-water, creating the brackish cave-pools that taniwha claim as permanent territories)
description: The cave-pool is still — perfectly still, in the way that only deep water in enclosed stone can be. The Kovaka dive-master kneels at the pool’s edge and presses her palm flat against the water’s surface without breaking it. She holds the position for thirty seconds. Then she withdraws her hand and nods. “It’s home,” she says. “Be respectful.” The water stirs. Something vast moves beneath the surface — a shape that the torchlight catches in fragments: a scaled ridge, a limb thick as a tree-tru...

name: Tendriculos
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — ravenous growth; the resonance of earth-fertility pushed to destructive excess, soil so rich and so hungry that everything planted in it grows beyond control and consumes everything around it
environment: Deepwood (the densest, darkest zones of the Great Forests — the old-growth interiors where the canopy blocks nearly all light and the undergrowth is so thick that navigation is by sound and touch rather than sight)
description: The Deepwood is dense — dense enough that sunlight reaches the forest floor only in thin, scattered beams, and the undergrowth is a tangle of moss, root, and fallen branch. The mound ahead looks like a compost-heap, green with new growth. It is not a compost-heap. The vines that compose it are moving, slowly, independently, and the opening at the mound’s centre — the one that looks like a hollow in the vegetation — is lined with thorns the length of daggers, and it is a mouth.

name: Tengu
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — canopy-wind; the resonance of wind in the highest forest, air channelled through branch-architecture into currents that only canopy-dwellers know, the Youngest Brother’s domain at its most refined and most playful
environment: High Canopy (the upper canopy of the Great Forests — specifically the Hasura canopy-settlement zones, where the forest-architecture of branch-platforms and vine-bridges creates an aerial environment that tengu navigate with natural ease)
description: The Hasura canopy-platform is a hundred feet above the forest floor, and the creature standing at the edge of the platform should not be able to stand there — the branch is too narrow for anything larger than a squirrel. But the tengu stands with the casual balance of something born to the air, its taloned feet gripping the bark with an ease that makes the Hasura sentinel beside you look clumsy by comparison. It is man-shaped — roughly — with a raven’s head, a raven’s beak, and wings folded a...

name: Tepegöz
flags: elemental_affinity:None — Dómnathar void; like all Dómnathar-forged creatures, a tepegöz registers as active absence on Kin-sense, the characteristic hollow signature of beings manufactured rather than born
environment: Dómnathar Tunnels (the wider passage-networks and excavation-chambers of active Dómnathar tunnel-systems — specifically the zones where tunnel construction has created spaces large enough for a 12-foot creature to move freely, or where the tepegöz has widened the tunnels itself through decades of habitation)
description: The tunnel is wider here — wider than Dómnathar standard, wider than Vestri bore-gauge, and the walls are scored with claw-marks that are too large and too deep for any creature you’ve encountered underground. The smell hits you next: rancid meat, old blood, and something underneath that is neither — the cold, mineral stink of something that has been living underground for a very long time without ever seeing sunlight. The thing that fills the tunnel ahead is twelve feet tall, broad as a cart...

name: Thoqqua
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire / Lord of Stone (Fire/Earth) — magma-worm; the resonance of rock melted by internal fire, the intersection of the Lady and the Lord’s domains expressed as a living geological process
environment: Scorchspires (the active volcanic zones, lava-tube networks, and geothermal fissure-fields of the Scorchspire range — anywhere the boundary between fire and earth is thinnest)
description: The volcanic fissure hisses — a sound like water on hot iron — and something worm-shaped pushes through the crack in the rock. It is three feet long, segmented, and glowing a dull orange-red, and the stone it touches melts. Not chars, not blackens — melts, flowing away from the creature’s body like candle-wax from a flame. It is a living drill-bit, and the volcanic vent it just emerged from was not here yesterday. The thoqqua made it.

name: Thunderbird
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of the Sea (Air/Water) — living storm; the resonance of a thunderstorm given physical form — not merely wind or rain or lightning but the entire atmospheric system compressed into a body of feather, talon, and fury
environment: Eternal Steppe (the vast open grasslands of the Mytroan — specifically the storm-corridors where continental weather-systems cross the steppe, the atmospheric zones where the Youngest Brother and the Lady of the Sea’s domains interact most violently)
description: The steppe-sky darkens — not the gradual darkening of a storm approaching but the sudden, absolute darkening of something vast passing between you and the sun. The shadow covers acres. The wingbeat that follows is not wind — it is thunder, a detonation of compressed air that flattens the grass for a hundred yards in every direction. The Mytroan wind-caller beside you drops to one knee, presses his forehead to the steppe-grass, and speaks a name. The name is old. The thing above you is older. ...

name: Tipua
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Lady of the Sea (Earth/Water) — landmark spirit; the resonance of specific geographical features that have persisted long enough to develop awareness — a boulder, a spring, a tree, a cliff-face — the elemental system acknowledging places that matter
environment: Kovaka Landmarks (the named geographical features of Kovaka coastal-forest territory — ancient trees, distinctive rock-formations, hot springs, river-forks, and cliff-edges that have served as navigational reference-points and cultural markers for centuries)
organization: Solitary (always bound to a specific landmark)
advancement: 8–10 HD (Medium); 11–14 HD (Large — the spirits of the most significant landmarks)
description: The landmark has always been here — the split boulder at the river-fork, the ancient pohutukawa tree at the cliff-edge, the hot spring where the valley narrows. The Kovaka have names for each one, and the names are older than the settlements. Today the landmark is different. The split boulder has shifted — not moved, but the gap between the halves is wider, and in the gap stands a woman who was not there a moment ago. She is beautiful, dark-haired, barefoot, and her eyes are the colour of the...

name: Tokoloshe
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — mischief-weight; a small, dense, heavy earth-resonance that feels disproportionately solid for the creature’s size — the Lord of Stone’s domain expressed as something that should not be as present as it is
environment: Warg Camps (the semi-permanent camp-grounds and seasonal gathering-sites of Warg pack territories on the Eternal Steppe — specifically the margins of Warg settlements where food-stores, sleeping-shelters, and goblin-quarter boundaries create the edges and in-between spaces that tokoloshe inhabit)
description: The Warg camp is quiet — the kind of quiet that happens after midnight when the fires are low and the pack-guards are watching the perimeter. Something small moves between the sleeping-shelters. It is knee-height, dark-skinned, hairless, and it walks with a rolling gait that suggests its legs are not quite the right length for its body. It carries a stone in one hand — a smooth, river-polished stone — and it is heading for the food-stores with the determined, focused tread of a creature that ...

name: Treant
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — ancient forest-foundation; the resonance of the earth beneath a forest’s oldest roots, soil that has been forest-floor for so long that the trees and the earth have become one system — not air (though the canopy moves in wind) but earth, the foundation from which all growth proceeds
environment: Great Forests (the old-growth hearts of the Great Forests — specifically the zones where trees have grown undisturbed for centuries, where the root-networks are deep and interconnected, and where the forest’s elemental identity is strongest)
description: The oak in the clearing is old — older than the settlement downstream, older than the Pasua patrol-routes that pass its roots, old enough that its trunk is wider than three people standing shoulder to shoulder. You have walked past it twice today. You did not notice the face. You notice it now because the face is looking at you, and the eyes — formed from knots in the bark, framed by ridges that were not there this morning — express a patience so vast and so slow that your entire life is, to ...

name: Troglodyte
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — shallow-cave; the resonance of limestone and mineral-earth near the surface, cool and damp, the Lord of Stone’s domain at its most accessible and least imposing
environment: Shallow Caves (the upper cave-systems, rock-shelters, and limestone karst formations found across Oreka — specifically the shallow cave-zones above Vestri mining-depth, where natural cave-systems provide shelter, moisture, and protection from surface weather)
organization: Clutch (2–5), squad (6–11 plus 1–2 monitors of 3 HD), or band (20–80 plus 20% noncombatants plus 3–13 monitors of 3 HD plus 1 chieftain of 3–6 HD)
description: The cave-mouth smells before you reach it. The stench is organic, chemical, and deliberately weaponised — a musk so thick it coats the back of your throat and makes your eyes water at thirty feet. The creatures that emerge from the smell are reptilian: bipedal, grey-green, scaled, with flat faces and cold eyes that regard you with a hostility that is not personal but absolute. You are warm-blooded. You do not belong in their caves. The stench intensifies. They are doing that on purpose.

name: Troll
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — feral vitality; the resonance of earth-as-regeneration, the Lord of Stone’s domain expressed as the relentless, unkillable persistence of stone — break it and it reforms, chip it and it grows back, erode it and it replaces itself
environment: Cold Mountains / Marshes (the northern Giant’s Teeth foothills, the cold marshes bordering the Eternal Steppe’s northern reaches, and the fog-wrapped mountain-valleys where trolls establish lairs — anywhere cold, wet, and remote enough to support a large predator that eats constantly)
description: The thing comes out of the marsh at a lope — six limbs of disproportionate length, a body like a collection of angles that someone forgot to soften, and a face that is all mouth. It is green, in the way that rot is green, and it smells like a compost-heap in summer, and it is very fast for something its size. But the thing about the troll — the thing that every survivor mentions first — is not the speed or the claws or the jaw. It is the arm you cut off that grows back. It is the wound that c...

name: Tsukumogami
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — object-soul; the resonance of an object that has been used, loved, and maintained for so long that the earth-element within its material has developed a rudimentary awareness — clay remembering hands, metal remembering forge-heat, wood remembering the tree
environment: Tanuki Groves (the communal lodge-complexes and craft-workshops of Tanuki settlements — specifically the older groves where objects have been in continuous use for decades, creating the conditions of sustained attention and care that produce tsukumogami)
advancement: 2–3 HD (Tiny); 4–6 HD (Small — tsukumogami of larger objects: wardrobes, looms, forge-bellows)
description: The teapot is dancing. You did not expect the teapot to dance. It is a perfectly ordinary ceramic teapot — well-made, well-used, chipped at the spout from decades of daily service — and it is executing a small, precise, delighted dance on the shelf, its lid clapping in rhythm. The Tanuki grandmother beside you is unsurprised. “That’s Katsu,” she says. “He was a teapot for forty years before he decided to be a person too. Best tea in the grove. Don’t ask about his opinions on water-temperature...

name: Tuchulcha
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — drowned malice; the resonance of water in places where it should not be — flooded cellars, submerged streets, drowned foundations — the Lady of the Sea’s domain claiming territory that was built for air, converting civilisation to ruin through patient inundation
environment: Drowned Places (the flood-damaged, water-logged, and permanently submerged structures of Eruskan river-cities — specifically the cellars, undercrofts, and lower storeys of buildings that have been partially or fully claimed by the Great River’s seasonal floods and never reclaimed by the living)
advancement: 9–12 HD (Medium); 13–16 HD (Large — tuchulcha of the largest drowned structures)
description: The drowned cellar is knee-deep in black water — river-water that has seeped through the foundation of the Eruskan merchant-house that has been abandoned since the flood. The water is still. The smell is mould and old wood and something underneath: sulphur, faint and sharp. The torch-light catches the surface of the water and the surface catches something back — a face, reflected from below, that is not your face. It has three faces, actually: one human, one serpent, one vulture, arranged on ...

name: Ugallu
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Lady of Fire (Earth/Fire) — desert-edge sovereign; the resonance of the transition zone where the Infinite Desert’s sun-baked sand meets the Giant’s Teeth’s volcanic stone — the Lord and Lady’s domains in arid, predatory equilibrium
environment: Desert Edge (the ridge-lines, rock-shelves, and boulder-fields at the transition between the Infinite Desert and the Giant’s Teeth foothills — specifically the caravan-routes and pass-mouths where travellers cross from sand to stone)
advancement: 9–12 HD (Large); 13–16 HD (Huge — the eldest desert-edge lords)
description: The desert edge is where the sand meets the stone — where the Infinite Desert’s dune-fields break against the Giant’s Teeth foothills like waves against a cliff. The creature standing on the ridge-line where sand becomes rock has the body of a lion and the head of a man, and the head is wrong. Not wrong the way a sphinx’s head is wrong (a sphinx looks composed, deliberate, designed); wrong the way a storm-cloud is wrong — massive, heavy-browed, bearded, with a mouth that could swallow a shiel...

name: Unicorn
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — forest-heart; the resonance of the deepest, purest forest-earth — soil that has never been ploughed, turned, or contaminated, the Lord of Stone’s domain expressed as the foundation of untouched wilderness
environment: Great Forests (the deepest, most pristine zones of the Great Forests — specifically the old-growth interiors where Kin settlements have never reached, where the forest-floor has been undisturbed for millennia, and where the elemental purity of the earth is absolute)
description: The forest clearing is lit from within — not by sunlight (the canopy is too thick) but by something else, a quality of light that has no visible source and no shadow. The creature standing in the clearing is a horse — or it looks like a horse, the way a cathedral looks like a building. It is white, impossibly white, the white of fresh snow before anyone has walked on it, and its mane falls like water. The horn is the colour of old ivory, spiralling from the centre of its forehead with the mat...

name: Uthikoloshe
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — shadow-current; the resonance of water in shadow, river-current that flows through the darkest places, the Lady of the Sea’s domain where her light does not reach — not corrupted water (like a spirit naga’s domain) but shadowed water, water that has become a medium for darkness
special_qualities: vulnerability to sunlight on dry land
environment: Warg Territory (the rivers, streams, and water-crossings of Warg pack territory on the Eternal Steppe — specifically the fords, shallow crossings, and river-bends where Warg packs cross between seasonal grazing-grounds, where the water is accessible and the uthikoloshe can ambush wading creatures)
description: The river-crossing is shallow — knee-deep, cold, and fast-moving. The Warg pack-scout wades in without hesitation and stops. The water around his ankles has darkened — not with mud but with something else, a shadow that has no source and no shape, a presence in the water that is cold in a way that river-water in summer should not be. The scout backs out slowly. “Uthikoloshe,” he says to the pack-leader. His voice is flat. “In the water. We go around.” The pack-leader looks at the river — the ...

name: Vampire Spawn
flags: elemental_affinity:None — a vampire spawn has no elemental resonance; it is dead matter animated by hunger, a body that feeds to sustain a semblance of existence without possessing any of the elemental connections that defined it in life
environment: Giant Tomb Complexes (the sealed burial-chambers, sarcophagus-halls, and crypt-networks of the Giant-era tomb complexes scattered through the Giant’s Teeth and Infinite Desert — places where the original Giants entombed their dead with rituals that preserved the bodies in conditions that, when combined with subsequent necromantic contamination, produced vampiric undead)
description: The tomb-air is dry and still and old, and the thing that steps out of the sarcophagus-shadow is not what you expected. You expected bone. You expected rot. What you get is a person — pale, sharp-featured, dressed in the remnants of burial-finery that has held together through centuries of absolute darkness. Its eyes are open and aware and utterly, coldly hungry, and when it smiles, the canines are longer than they should be, and the smile does not contain a single molecule of warmth. It is n...

name: Vanth
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords — grave-stillness; the resonance of all four elements settling into equilibrium, the moment where fire cools, water stills, wind falls silent, and earth receives — not the harmonious balance of the qilin or the learned wisdom of the simurgh, but the quiet cessation that follows the end of a life, when the elements that comprised a living being return to the world
environment: Battlefields (the sites of mass Kin death — battlefields, massacre-sites, plague-graves, and disaster-zones, specifically within the first days or weeks after death, when the recently dead have not yet been fully committed to the earth and the boundary between life and death is thinnest; most commonly documented at Eruskan military engagements)
advancement: 7–10 HD (Medium); 11–14 HD (Large — the greater vanth, which appear at the largest mass-death sites)
description: The battlefield is three days old. The crows have come and gone. The burial-detail has done what it can. And yet something remains — a figure standing at the field’s edge where the dead are thickest, motionless, masked, carrying a staff that is neither weapon nor walking-stick but something between. It is the height of a tall man but too thin, wrapped in a cloak the colour of old bronze, and its face is a mask — featureless, smooth, with no eyes and no mouth but a surface that somehow conveys...

name: Vodyanoy
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — river-lord; the resonance of a specific stretch of river claimed by an intelligence that manages it — not corrupted water (like a spirit naga’s domain) and not shadowed water (like an uthikoloshe’s crossing) but governed water, river that has a master who keeps it healthy, productive, and under absolute personal control
environment: Great River / Kailian Deep (the deep pools, bridge-hollows, and river-bends of the Great River and its major tributaries, and the freshwater deeps of the Kailian Sea coast — specifically the pools beneath bridges, dams, and river-structures where the water is deep enough and permanent enough to sustain a vodyanoy’s territorial claim)
organization: Solitary or court (1 vodyanoy plus 2–4 water fey attendants — lasa, water mephits, or small water elementals)
advancement: 11–15 HD (Large); 16–20 HD (Huge — the eldest river-lords of the deepest pools)
description: The river-pool beneath the bridge is deep — deeper than the river should be at this point, the bottom invisible even in clear water. Something lives in the depth, and the Eruskan rivermen know what it is because the bridge has been here for four hundred years and the thing below the bridge has been here longer. It surfaces at dusk. It is man-shaped — roughly — with a face like a catfish given human proportions: wide-mouthed, bearded with trailing whisker-tendrils, and green-skinned, covered i...

name: Water Elemental, Small
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water)
environment: Any body of water; most common in the Great River and its tributaries, the Kailian and Apelian Seas, and the Deepwater Marches
description: The river rises. Not floods — the water level does not change — but a column of river-water pulls itself upright, shaping limbs and a torso and something that might be a head from the current itself. It stands on the river’s surface like a person stands on a floor, and within its translucent body you can see river-debris turning slowly: pebbles, weeds, a small fish swimming in confused circles. When it moves, the sound is rain on stone.

name: Water Elemental, Medium
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water)
environment: As Small Water Elemental
description: The river rises. Not floods — the water level does not change — but a column of river-water pulls itself upright, shaping limbs and a torso and something that might be a head from the current itself. It stands on the river’s surface like a person stands on a floor, and within its translucent body you can see river-debris turning slowly: pebbles, weeds, a small fish swimming in confused circles. name: Water Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — mist-mischief; the Lady’s domain at its most trivial and annoying
environment: Great River (river-banks, bridge-hollows, canal-sides, and anywhere the Great River generates mist)
description: The river-mist thickens — unnaturally, in a column that resolves into a grinning, dripping, knee-high figure with wings like wet sails and a face that is all malicious delight. It spits a stream of acid-tinged water at your boot and cackles.

name: Water Naga
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) — guardian; unlike the spirit naga’s corrupted-water, a water naga’s resonance is protective — river-current that watches, water that guards
environment: Great River Pools (the deep, still pools along the Great River’s middle stretch — specifically the naturally occurring pools where the river slows and deepens, creating habitats that water nagas claim as territories and guard with territorial but not malicious intent)
description: The pool-water is clear — deceptively clear, the kind of clarity that means nothing has been disturbing the bottom for a long time. The naga is coiled on the submerged ledge like a crown of emerald-green scales, its humanoid face composed, watchful, and radiating an intelligence that is not hostile but is absolutely, unmistakably evaluating you. It is deciding whether you are a threat, a resource, or an interesting conversation.

name: Wendigo
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — frozen, corrupted; the resonance of wind stripped of warmth, the Youngest Brother’s playful domain twisted into predatory cold — not the natural cold of winter but the hungry cold of something that has consumed all warmth in its vicinity and still wants more
environment: Gatefall / Kinsweave Forests (the deep, cold forests of Gatefall Reach and the northern Kinsweave border-kingdoms — specifically the zones where Dómnathar influence has thinned the elemental fabric and winter persists longer than it should, where the boundary between natural cold and supernatural hunger is thinnest)
advancement: 13–18 HD (Large); 19–24 HD (Huge — the eldest and most terrible)
description: The hunger comes before the creature does. You feel it first in your stomach — a hollow, gnawing emptiness that has nothing to do with whether you’ve eaten — and then in your mind, where it becomes something worse: the certainty that nothing will ever fill you, that the emptiness is permanent, and that the only thing that might — might — ease it is the warmth of another living body, broken open. The trees above creak. Something enormous moves through the canopy with a speed that should be imp...

name: Wight
flags: elemental_affinity:None — a wight is elemental absence, the dead animated by hunger without any of the elemental identity that defined them in life
environment: Any (barrows, crypts, battlefields, and sealed burial-chambers across Oreka — wherever the dead were interred without proper rites or where necromantic contamination has reached preserved corpses)
description: The barrow opens into a chamber that has not been touched by light since it was sealed. The figure inside is standing — not lying, not seated, but standing against the back wall with its arms at its sides and its eyes open. It was Kin once. You can tell by the bone-structure, the remnants of clothing, the corroded weapon still gripped in one hand. But the eyes are wrong — flat, lightless, and lit from within by something that is not life. It sees you. It steps forward. The temperature drops.

name: Will-O’-Wisp
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) — deceptive; the resonance of air that misleads, the Youngest Brother’s domain at its most treacherous — not the honest wind of the steppe but the false light of the marsh, air that lies
environment: Deepwater Marches (the deep-bog zones, reed-mazes, and flooded forests of the Deepwater Marches — specifically the treacherous areas where solid ground and bottomless bog are indistinguishable and where a single wrong step means drowning)
description: The marsh-light appears at dusk — a pale, bobbing globe of blue-white luminescence that drifts between the reed-beds with a movement that looks deliberate. It looks like a lantern. It looks like a signal. It looks like someone is ahead of you, navigating the marsh, showing you the safe path. Follow it. Follow it deeper into the bog, where the ground becomes water and the water becomes mud and the mud closes over your ankles, your knees, your waist. The light stops. It turns. And you understan...

name: Winter Wolf
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of the Sea (Air/Water) — winter predator; the resonance of cold wind and frozen water combined, the two elements meeting in the winter-state that produces ice, frost, and the killing cold of the northern steppe
environment: Northern Steppe (the cold grasslands and snow-covered plains of the Eternal Steppe’s northern reaches — the border-zone between the steppe and the northern mountain-barrier where winters are long and wolves are large)
description: The wolf on the ridge is white — white as the snow-field it stands on, invisible except for the eyes (amber, steady) and the breath (which is not breath but a thin, constant stream of ice-fog that curls from its jaws like smoke from a forge). It is larger than any wolf should be — shoulder-height to a tall man — and it watches you with an intelligence that wolves do not possess. When it speaks — and it will speak, because winter wolves speak — the voice is a low, grinding rasp, and the words ...

name: Wolf
flags: elemental_affinity:Species Harmony — wolves carry the species-specific resonance of their kind, distinct from elemental Kin-sense but part of the world’s ecological web
environment: Forests / Steppe (the Great Forests’ edges and the Eternal Steppe — anywhere pack-hunting in open or semi-open terrain is viable)
description: Medium Animal

name: Worg
flags: elemental_affinity:Wild Static — worgs are intelligent non-Kin creatures that register on Kin-sense as unreadable presence, the same wild-static signature shared by all non-Kin sapients (goblins, goblinoids, Wargs); recognisable, alive, but not elementally identifiable
environment: Any (worgs range across all of Oreka’s temperate and cold regions — Eternal Steppe, Great Forest edges, mountain foothills, and the margins of every settled area)
description: The wolf is too large, too intelligent, and looking at you with an expression that wolves do not have. Wolves assess threats. This creature is assessing you — your armour, your weapons, the way you hold your sword. It understands equipment. It understands threat-hierarchy. And when it speaks — growling, accented Common — it says: “Move. Or I move you.”

name: Wraith
flags: elemental_affinity:None — a wraith’s resonance is burned absence, the elemental signature of fire-death — not the extinguished-fire of the Ashen Hollow spectres but the burned-away identity of creatures that died in fire, their elemental connections consumed along with their bodies
environment: Burned Cities (the fire-ruined settlements, razed towns, and burned-out strongholds scattered across Oreka — specifically the Reckoning-era burned cities of the Kinsweave and Tidebloom Reach, where the Dómnathar’s surface campaigns used fire as a primary destruction-tool, and where the mass fire-death produced concentrations of wraith-spawning energy)
organization: Solitary, pair, or gang (3–5 wraiths, sometimes led by 1 dread wraith)
description: The burned city is dark — dark in the way that fire-ruins are dark, the charred beams absorbing torchlight, the collapsed roofs creating pockets of absolute blackness that no flame seems to penetrate. Something moves in the blackness — not a shadow (shadows are passive, projected by light) but something that moves as darkness, a shape of concentrated absence that slides between the ruins with deliberate, predatory intent. Where it passes, the air goes cold, and the coldness is not temperature...

name: Wyvern
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of Fire (Air/Fire) — warm-hill predator; the resonance of warm updraughts rising from sun-heated hillsides, the air-fire intersection that produces the thermal columns wyverns ride to altitude — not the intellectual fire of dragons or the sacred wind of thunderbirds, but the simple, functional heat that a predator uses to fly
environment: Warm Hills (the sun-heated hill-country and low ridgelines of central and southern Oreka — specifically the foothills where thermal updraughts are reliable, where the terrain provides cliff-nesting sites and the plains below provide prey, and where the warm-season climate supports year-round flying)
description: The shape in the sky resolves as it banks — two legs, two wings, a barbed tail longer than its body, and a head that is all jaw and no subtlety. It is not a dragon. It does not have a dragon’s intelligence, a dragon’s breath, or a dragon’s treasure-hoard ambition. What it has is a tail-stinger loaded with enough venom to drop a horse, a wingspan that lets it carry that horse into the sky, and the single-minded predatory focus of a creature that has identified you as the right size for dinner.

name: Yer-Sub
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — deep-soil guardian; the resonance of the bedrock layer beneath the steppe, the Lord of Stone’s domain in its nurturing, foundation-providing aspect — the earth that supports all growth, all roots, all life on the surface
environment: Eternal Steppe (Mytroan) — the deep grasslands, seasonal migration routes, and sacred earth-sites where the steppe’s bedrock approaches the surface; yer-sub manifest at locations of geological significance: aquifer convergence-points, natural springs, and places where the stone-root is strongest
advancement: 11–15 HD (Large); 16–20 HD (Huge — the deepest, oldest yer-sub, whose forms incorporate fossil-stone and aquifer-crystal)
description: The steppe splits. Not violently — there is no earthquake, no crack of breaking stone — but a seam appears in the earth as if the ground were opening a door. From within rises a figure of compressed soil, clay, and living stone: humanoid in outline but faceless, broad as a wall, and rooted to the earth by tendrils of bedrock that trail from its legs like a gown. It smells of rain-wet soil and deep stone. When it moves, the steppe moves with it — grass leans toward it, stones shift, and for a ...

name: Yuki-Onna
flags: elemental_affinity:None — a yuki-onna’s resonance is frozen grief, undeath shaped by cold, the elemental connections of the original person iced over and preserved but inaccessible, like a voice heard through a frozen windowpane
alignment: Usually neutral evil (but see below — some yuki-onna retain fragments of their living personality and may spare travellers under specific conditions)
environment: Northern Forests (Hasura) — the snow-deep forests and mountain-passes of the Hasura homelands, where winter is long and the dead do not always stay buried beneath the snow — specifically the high passes, blizzard-routes, and deep-winter trails where travellers are most vulnerable to exposure
advancement: 7–9 HD (Medium); 10–12 HD (Medium — the eldest, most powerful, whose frozen embrace can kill in a single round)
description: The snow falls harder after midnight, and the trail disappears. You are not lost — you know exactly where you are — but the cold has reached the place inside your chest where decisions live, and you cannot quite remember why you were walking. You should sit down. You should rest. The snow is soft. The cold is not painful anymore; it is becoming warm, which you know — in the part of your mind that is still thinking — means you are dying. And then she is there: standing in the snow without foot...

name: Zashiki-Warashi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) — hearthstone warmth; the resonance of a well-built house, a foundation laid true, earth-in-its-sheltering-aspect — not the deep bedrock of the yer-sub but the domestic, nurturing earth that makes a house a home
environment: Tanuki Lodges (the longhouses, inns, and communal halls of Tanuki communities — specifically well-maintained, long-established structures where generations of care have created the conditions for fey habitation; zashiki-warashi never appear in new buildings, temporary shelters, or neglected structures)
advancement: 2–3 HD (Small — longer residence deepens the blessing)
description: You will not see it. That is the first thing to understand. You will hear it — small feet on wooden floors, a child’s giggle behind a screen, the sound of something being moved in a room where no one is standing. You will feel it — the house is warm, the food tastes better, the neighbours are kind. The garden grows. The baby sleeps through the night. The family prospers. And you will never, not once, see the zashiki-warashi — but it is there, has always been there, and the moment it leaves, y...

name: Zmey Gorynych
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) — volcanic intelligence; the resonance of fire at its most calculating, the Lady’s domain in its predatory, thinking aspect — not the mindless burn of a wildfire or the nurturing warmth of a forge-bond but the deliberate, patient, strategic heat of magma that chooses where to flow
environment: Scorchspires (Rarozhki) — the deep volcanic valleys, caldera-rims, and magma-lake caverns of the Scorchspires’ inner range — specifically the zones too hot for any creature without fire immunity, where the zmey gorynych lairs in comfort while everything else is kept at bay by temperature alone
advancement: 16–20 HD (Huge); 21–30 HD (Gargantuan — the eldest, whose three breath weapons can devastate an army)
description: Three heads. That is what the Rarozhki scouts reported, and three heads is what you see: three serpentine necks rising from a body built like a fortress of red-black scales, each head armoured, horned, and breathing independently. The left head watches you. The centre head watches the sky. The right head watches the tunnel behind it. Nothing approaches a zmey gorynych from a single direction, because the zmey gorynych is watching every direction simultaneously. Each mouth trails smoke. Each e...

name: Zombie
flags: elemental_affinity:None — a zombie’s resonance is absence, like a wight’s, but less — a wight hungers, a zombie does not even have that; it is the absolute minimum of undeath, meat animated by the thinnest thread of necromantic energy, registering on Kin-sense as “dead thing moving” with no further complexity
environment: Any (zombies appear wherever necromantic contamination meets preserved corpses — battlefields, mass graves, Dómnathar-influenced zones, and locations where the boundary between life and death has been thinned by catastrophe or dark magic)
description: It walks. That is the horror of it — not the rot, not the smell, not the exposed bone or the grey-green skin or the blank, staring eyes. The horror is that it walks. It was a person. You can see the person it was — the build, the clothing, the face. And now it walks toward you with its arms out and its mouth open and no thought behind the eyes at all. Nothing. Not hunger, not malice, not pain. Nothing. It is meat that remembers how to move and has forgotten everything else.
